---
title: "1_nuseds_collation"
author: "Bruno S. Carturan, Eric Hertz, Stephanie J. Peacock"
# date: "2025-08-22"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true                  # Adds a table of contents to the document
    toc_float: true           # Makes the table of contents float on the side as the reader scrolls.
    toc_collapsed: true        # Starts the table of contents in a collapsed state.
    toc_depth: 3               # Specifies the depth of headers (e.g., ##, ###) to include in the table of contents.
    number_sections: true      # 
    theme: journal  # lumen
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE) 
```

The goal of the script is to merge the two constituent datasets **all_areas_nuseds** (referred to as **NUSEDS**) and **conservation_unit_census_sites** (referred to as **CUSS**) so each time series in **NUSEDS** (i.e., fish counts associated to a unique populations, characterized by a unique `IndexId`/`POP_ID` & `GFE_ID` association) can be attributed to a unique conservation unit (CU) in **CUSS** (characterized by `CU_NAME` and `FULL_CU_IN`). But there are multiple inconsistencies between the two datasets that lead to discard a significant amount of data points in **NUSEDS**, unless fixes are implemented. This script is a attempt to implement such fixes.

The dataset version corresponding to this script is:

Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. NuSEDS - New Salmon Escapement Database System. Pacific Biological Station. Retrieved January 22, 2024, from <https://open.canada.ca/data/en/dataset/c48669a3-045b-400d-b730-48aafe8c5ee6>.

**IMPORTANT NOTE:** This is the script that PSF maintains actively and it is updated annually as new versions of NuSEDS are released. The corresponding script used in [Atkinson et al. 2025. Monitoring for fisheries or for fish? Declines in monitoring of salmon spawners continue despite a conservation crisis](https://doi.org/10.1139/cjfas-2024-0387) is accessible at: [a_nuseds_collation.Rmd](https://github.com/salmonwatersheds/salmon_data_demise/blob/main/code/a_nuseds_collation.Rmd).


```{r, include = F}

#'******************************************************************************
#' The goal of the script is to import, clean and format NuSEDS data.
#' Script based on Emma Atkinson's previous version (26-Mar-2019).
#' 
#' Imported files:

#'- all_areas_nuseds_DATE.csv                    # "NUSEDS"
#'- conservation_unit_census_sites_DATE.csv      # "CUSS"
#'- conservationunits_decoder.csv                      # the list of conservation units as defined in the PSE
#'- nuseds_report_definitions.csv                      # definition of the fields in all_areas_nuseds
#'- DFO_All_Streams_Segments_20240408.xlsx             # stream - GFE_ID data file from DFO (emailed by Wu Zhipeng, DFO, 09/04/2024)
#'- PSF_modified_SEP_releases_2023.xlsx                # DFO's Hatchery dataset (emailed by Brock Ramshaw, DFO, 03/07/2023)
#'- /pse_conservation_units/pse_conservation_units.gdb # shape files of the conservation unites as defined in the PSE
#'- /se_boundary_regions/se_boundary_regions.shp       # shape files for the Region boundaries as defined in the PSE


#' Files both imported & exported (intermediate "_TEMP" files created to save computation time):

#'- all_areas_nuseds_noNA0s_TEMP.csv  # NUSEDS after removing the time series only made of NAs and/or 0s (not used in paper)
#'- all_areas_nuseds_noNAs_TEMP.csv   # NUSEDS after removing the time series only made of NAs (used in paper)
#'- trackRecord_noNA0s_TEMP.csv       # all the time series in CUSS not in NUSEDS using all_areas_nuseds_noNA0s_TEMP.csv (not used in paper)
#'- trackRecord_noNAs_TEMP.csv        # all the time series in CUSS not in NUSEDS using all_areas_nuseds_noNAs_TEMP.csv (used in paper)


#' Files exported:

#'- 1_NuSEDS_escapement_data_collated_DATE.csv   # the cleaned combined NUSEDS and CUSS dataset with time series containing only NAs removed
#'- 1_series_inNUSEDS_noInCUSS_DATE.csv          # Export the series in NUSEDS with IndexIds and GFE_IDs not in CUSS with info on the number of data points and POPULATION
#'- 1_series_removed_DATE.csv                    # series removed to either NUSEDS or CUSS
#'- 1_series_added_DATE.csv                      # series added to either NUSEDS or CUSS
#' - log_file.csv                                     # the log file containing the name of the orginak NUSEDS and CUSS files imported and the information about 0s being removed or not.

#'******************************************************************************

# NOTE (to remove eventually): original script is:
# 1_nuseds_data_collationJun72023.R in:
# \X Drive\1_PROJECTS\1_Active\Fraser_VIMI\analysis\Compilation\Code

rm(list = ls())
graphics.off()

# Loading packages & functions
library(tidyr)
library(plyr)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(parallel)
library(sp) # to calculate distances from geo-spatial coordinates
library(sf)
library(scales)
# library(dplyr)
# library(tibble)
# library(scales)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(reshape2)
# library(stringr)
# library(viridis)

# reset the wd to head using the location of the current script
path <- rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path
dirhead <- "population-indicators"
path_ahead <- sub(pattern = paste0("\\",dirhead,".*"),replacement = "", x = path)
wd_head <- paste0(path_ahead,dirhead)
setwd(wd_head)

# Now import functions related to directories.
# Note that the script cannot be called again once the directory is set to the 
# subdirectory of the project (unless setwd() is called again).
source("code/functions_set_wd.R")
source("code/functions_general.R")

# return the name of the directories for the different projects:
subDir_projects <- subDir_projects_fun()

wds_l <- set_working_directories_fun(subDir = subDir_projects$spawner_surveys,
                                     Export_locally = F)
wd_head <- wds_l$wd_head
wd_project <- wds_l$wd_project
wd_code <- wds_l$wd_code
wd_data <- wds_l$wd_data
wd_figures <- wds_l$wd_figures
wd_output <- wds_l$wd_output
wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS <- wds_l$wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS

wd_references_dropbox <- paste(wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS,
                               wds_l$wd_project_dropbox,
                               "references",sep="/")

wd_data_dropbox <- paste(wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS,
                         wds_l$wd_project_dropbox,
                         "data",sep="/")

wd_documents <- paste(wd_project,"documents",sep="/")

wd_pop_indic_data_input_dropbox <- paste(wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS,
                                         wds_l$wd_population_indicator_data_input_dropbox,
                                         sep = "/")

wd_hatchery <- paste(wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS,
                     wds_l$wd_population_indicator_dropbox,
                     "hatchery-releases",
                     sep = "/")

wd_pop_indic_data_gis_dropbox <- gsub("input","gis",wd_pop_indic_data_input_dropbox)

source("code/functions.R")

options(digits = 9)


#'* !!! DECISIONS TO MAKE !!! *

# Set tp true to update the datasets
export_datasets <- T

# Decision in ## Remove the IndexId & GFE_ID time series in NUSEDS with only NAs and/or 0s
# If TRUE, time series only composed of NAs and/or 0s are removed from NUSEDS; 
# If FALSE, time series only composed of NAs are removed from NUSEDS.
remove_timeSeries_zeros_too <- F

# Used just after NUSEDS and CUSS are merged: to decide if we want to replace
# all the remaining 0s by NAs.
#' TODO: this should NOT have to be done in this script!
# replace_zeros_byNAs <- F

# To avoid having to wait too much time when executing the code multiple times.
# Should be set to FALSE by default.
import_file_locally_TEMPORARY <- T

# To prevent different locations (with different GFE_ID) to have exactly the same coordinates
update_coordinates <- T
```

# Import datasets

The **NUSEDS** (all_areas_nuseds) and **CUSS** (conservation_unit_census_sites) datasets are imported and duplicated rows are removed. The columns considered for NuSEDS are related to the population, location, fish counts and year of assessment:

```{r,echo=FALSE}
#'* Import the All Areas NuSEDS data ("NUSEDS) *

#' Previously all_areas_nuseds
# all_areas_nuseds <- datasets_NuSEDS_fun(name_dataSet = "all_areas_nuseds", 
#                                         from_NuSEDS_website = F, 
#                                          wd = wd_data_dropbox)

#' The date at the end of the file name should be the date of the update of NuSEDS 
#' on the DFO's website, not the date the file was downloaded.
all_areas_nuseds <- import_mostRecent_file_fun(wd = wd_data_dropbox, 
                                               pattern = "All Areas NuSEDS") # previously: "all_areas_nuseds"

# Record the name of the all_areas_nuseds file name for the log file: 
files_c <- list.files(wd_data_dropbox)
files_c <- files_c[grepl(x = files_c, pattern = "All Areas NuSEDS")] # previously "all_areas_nuseds"
file.mtime <- file.mtime(paste(wd_data_dropbox,files_c,sep="/"))
all_areas_nuseds_file_name <- files_c[file.mtime == max(file.mtime)]
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
#'* Import Conservation Unit Census Sites ("CUCS") *

#' Previously conservation_unit_system_sites (CUSS)
# DFO provided files matching streams and Nuseds to full CU index 
# conservation_unit_system_sites <- datasets_NuSEDS_fun(name_dataSet = "conservation_unit_system_sites", 
#                                                       from_NuSEDS_website = F, 
#                                                       wd = wd_data_dropbox)

#' The date at the end of the file name should be the date of the update of NuSEDS 
#' on the DFO's website, not the date the file was downloaded.
conservation_unit_census_sites <- import_mostRecent_file_fun(wd = wd_data_dropbox, 
                                                             pattern = "Conservation Unit Census Sites") # previously: "conservation_unit_census_sites"


#' check for duplicated rows: --> NONE
# dupli <- conservation_unit_census_sites %>%
#   dplyr::group_by(SPECIES_QUALIFIED,POP_ID,GFE_ID) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(n = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
#   dplyr::filter(n > 1L)

# Record the name of the conservation_unit_census_sites file name for the log file: 
files_c <- list.files(wd_data_dropbox)
files_c <- files_c[grepl(x = files_c, pattern = "Conservation Unit Census Sites")] # previously conservation_unit_census_sites
file.mtime <- file.mtime(paste(wd_data_dropbox,files_c,sep="/"))
conservation_unit_census_sites_file_name <- files_c[file.mtime == max(file.mtime)]

# Add column coordinates_changed in case coordinates are added (if missing) or modified
conservation_unit_census_sites$coordinates_changed <- F
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
#'* Import the definition of the different fields of these two datasets *
fields_def <- nuseds_fields_definitions_fun(wd_references = wd_references_dropbox)
# fields_def$all_areas_nuseds$AREA
# fields_def$cu_system_sites$IS_INDICATOR
#' TODO UPDATE

#'* Import list for the fields in NUSEDS and CUSS that are associated to unique IndexId and GFE_ID *
#' IndexId is define below as the species acronym + _ + POP_ID
#' Those are used to updated all the columns/fields in NUSEDS and CUSS when adding
#' or updating a population. 
#' TODO UPDATE
fields_l <- fields_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                      conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites)

# fields_l$NUSEDS$IndexId

#'* Create a dataframe with the species names and the different acronymes *
sp_salmon_detail <- c("Chum", "Chinook", "Coho", "Pink even","Pink odd","Sockeye lake","Sockeye river")
sp_salmon <-        c("Chum", "Chinook", "Coho", "Pink","Pink","Sockeye","Sockeye")
sp_salmon_acro <-     c("CM", "CK", "CO", "PKE", "PKO", "SEL", "SER")
sp_salmon_acro_ncc <- c("CM", "CN", "CO", "PKE", "PKO",  "SX",  "SX")  # SpeciesId ; NCC Salmon Database (NCCSDB) Designation 

sp_salmon_names_acro_df <- data.frame(name = sp_salmon,
                                      name_detail = sp_salmon_detail,
                                      acronym = sp_salmon_acro,
                                      acronym_ncc = sp_salmon_acro_ncc)
```

The list of conservation units (CUs) as shown in the Pacific Salmon Explorer ([PSE](https://www.salmonexplorer.ca/)) is imported. The columns `cu_name_pse` and `cu_name_dfo` correspond to `CU_NAME` in **CUSS**, `cu_index` corresponds to `FULL_CU_INDEX`.

```{r, include=FALSE}
#'* Import the PSE list of CUs (conservationunits_decoder) *
fromDatabase <- F
update_file_csv <- F
conservationunits_decoder <- datasets_database_fun(nameDataSet = "conservationunits_decoder.csv",
                                                   fromDatabase = fromDatabase,
                                                   update_file_csv = update_file_csv,
                                                   wd = wd_pop_indic_data_input_dropbox)

colnames(conservationunits_decoder)[colnames(conservationunits_decoder) == "species_abbr"] <- "species_qualified"

# temporary fix to do (until the source file is corrected)
d_fixes <- data.frame(cuid = c(528,756,758,759,760,761,763,1022),
                      cu_index_old = c("SX_528","","","","","","","SER-023"),
                      cu_index_new = c("SEL-16-01","SEL-03-07","SEL-05-01","SEL-06-19",
                                       "SEL-09-04","SEL-09-05","SEL-10-02","SER-23"))

for(r in 1:nrow(d_fixes)){
  cuid <- d_fixes$cuid[r]
  cu_index_new <- d_fixes$cu_index_new[r]
  cond <- conservationunits_decoder$cuid == cuid
  conservationunits_decoder$cu_index[cond] <- cu_index_new
}

d_fixes
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
head(conservationunits_decoder[,c("region","species_name","species_qualified","cuid","cu_name_pse","cu_name_dfo","cu_index")])
```

The **DFO file of the stream locations and coordinates** (emailed from Wu Zhipeng, DFO, 09/04/2024) is imported. The columns `NME` and `ID` correspond to `SYSTEM_SITE` and `GFE_ID` in CUSS, respectively.

```{r, echo=FALSE}
#'* Import streamlocationids *
# streamlocationids <- datasets_database_fun(nameDataSet = "streamlocationids.csv",
#                                            fromDatabase = fromDatabase,
#                                            update_file_csv = update_file_csv,
#                                            wd = wd_pop_indic_data_input_dropbox)

#'* Import the stream - GFE_ID data file from DFO *
#' (emailed from Wu Zhipeng, DFO, 09/04/2024)
#' There are different locations in the 2nd first sheets to we combine them into a unique dataframe
DFO_All_Streams_Segments <- read_xlsx(paste0(wd_data_dropbox,"/DFO_All_Streams_Segments_20240408.xlsx"),
                                      sheet = "All Streams")

DFO_All_Streams_Segments2 <- read_xlsx(paste0(wd_data_dropbox,"/DFO_All_Streams_Segments_20240408.xlsx"),
                                      sheet = "Stream Segments")

# Emailed as "GFE_IDs.xlsx"
# DFO_All_Streams_Segments3 <- read_xlsx(paste0(wd_data_dropbox,"/DFO_GFE_IDs_list_1.xlsx"),
#                                       sheet = "Export Worksheet")

# Emailed as "GFE_IDs_20240305.xlsx"
# DFO_All_Streams_Segments4 <- read_xlsx(paste0(wd_data_dropbox,"/DFO_GFE_IDs_list_2.xlsx"),
#                                        sheet = "Export Worksheet")

columns <- c("NME","ID","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")
DFO_All_Streams_Segments <- rbind(DFO_All_Streams_Segments[,columns],
                                  DFO_All_Streams_Segments2[,columns]
                                  # DFO_All_Streams_Segments3[,columns],
                                  # DFO_All_Streams_Segments4[,columns]
                                  )
DFO_All_Streams_Segments <- unique(DFO_All_Streams_Segments)

rm(DFO_All_Streams_Segments2)
head(DFO_All_Streams_Segments)

```

The **DFO hatchery data file** (emailed from Brock Ramshaw, DFO, 03/07/2023) is imported because it contains `GFE_ID` (i.e., `STOCK_GFE_ID` and `REL_GFE_ID`) and coordinates that are missing in the above DFO stream location file:

```{r, include=FALSE}
#'* Import DFO's Hatchery dataset * 
# because there are coordinates available there for
# certain GFE_ID that are not in DFO_All_Streams_Segments
DFO_hatchery <- read_xlsx(paste0(wd_hatchery,"/data/PSF_modified_SEP_releases_2023.xlsx"),
                         sheet = 1)

DFO_hatchery1 <- DFO_hatchery[,c("STOCK_GFE_ID","STOCK_WATERBODY_NAME","STOCK_LATITUDE","STOCK_LONGITUDE")]
cond_NA <- is.na(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_GFE_ID) | 
  is.na(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_LATITUDE) |
  is.na(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_LONGITUDE)
DFO_hatchery1 <- DFO_hatchery1[!cond_NA,]
DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_LATITUDE <- round(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_LATITUDE,6)
DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_LONGITUDE <- round(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_LONGITUDE,6)
DFO_hatchery1 <- unique(DFO_hatchery1)
nrow(DFO_hatchery1) # 535

DFO_hatchery2 <- DFO_hatchery[,c("REL_GFE_ID","REL_WATERBODY_NAME","REL_LATITUDE","REL_LONGITUDE")]
DFO_hatchery2 <- DFO_hatchery2[!is.na(DFO_hatchery2$REL_GFE_ID),]
DFO_hatchery2$REL_LATITUDE <- round(DFO_hatchery2$REL_LATITUDE,6)
DFO_hatchery2$REL_LONGITUDE <- round(DFO_hatchery2$REL_LONGITUDE,6)
DFO_hatchery2 <- unique(DFO_hatchery2)
nrow(DFO_hatchery2) # 1423

sum(duplicated(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_GFE_ID)) # 19
sum(duplicated(DFO_hatchery2$REL_GFE_ID)) # 87

GFE_ID_dupli <- unique(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_GFE_ID[duplicated(DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_GFE_ID)])
d <- DFO_hatchery1[DFO_hatchery1$STOCK_GFE_ID %in% GFE_ID_dupli,]
d <- d[order(d$STOCK_GFE_ID),]

rm(DFO_hatchery1)
rm(DFO_hatchery2)
rm(d)

#' There are inconsistencies and I do not want to spend too much time fixing them,
#' so I won't merge DFO_hatchery with DFO_All_Streams_Segments.
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}
head(DFO_hatchery[,c("STOCK_GFE_ID","STOCK_WATERBODY_NAME","STOCK_LATITUDE","STOCK_LONGITUDE",
                     "REL_GFE_ID","REL_WATERBODY_NAME","REL_LATITUDE","REL_LONGITUDE")])
```

```{r, include=F}
# streamlocationids$sys_nm <- tolower(streamlocationids$sys_nm)
# streamlocationids$cu_name_pse <- tolower(streamlocationids$cu_name_pse)
# 
# # Add species_name to streamlocationids
# cuids <- unique(streamlocationids$cuid)
# streamlocationids$species_name <- NA
# for(c in cuids){
#   cond <- conservationunits_decoder$cuid == c
#   if(sum(cond) > 0){
#     streamlocationids$species_name[streamlocationids$cuid == c] <- conservationunits_decoder$species_name[cond]
#   }
# }
```

The regions and CUs shape files as defined in the PSE are imported:

```{r, include=FALSE}

#'* Import the shape files for the Region boundaries *
#' Note that se_boundary/se_boundary.shp is used in 0_assign-regions.R. This is a
#' lighter shape file but contains the same regions. The code here works with 
#' se_boundary.shp.
# regions_shp <- st_read(paste0(wd_pop_indic_data_gis_dropbox,"/se_boundary_regions/se_boundary_regions.shp")) %>%
#   st_transform(crs = 4269)
# unique(regions_shp$regionname)
wd_geo_regions <- gsub("1_PROJECTS","5_DATA/Mapping/study areas/shapefiles/PSE_regions/",wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS)

regions_shp <- st_read(paste0(wd_geo_regions,"se_boundary_regions_simple/se_boundary_regions_simple.shp")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = 4269)
unique(regions_shp$Region)

sf_use_s2(FALSE) # so that st_intersects() and st_simplify() can be used

# regions_shp_full <- regions_shp
# regions_shp <- st_simplify(x = regions_shp, dTolerance = .002) # .001 # to reduce computation time
# 
# plot(st_geometry(regions_shp_full[1,]))
# plot(st_geometry(regions_shp[1,]))

#'* Import the geodatabase for the CU boundaries  *
# CUs_gdb <- st_read(paste0(wd_pop_indic_data_gis_dropbox,"/pse_conservation_units/pse_conservation_units.gdb")) %>%
#   st_transform(crs = 4269)

wd_geo_cus <- gsub("1_PROJECTS","5_DATA/CUs_Master/GDB/",wd_X_Drive1_PROJECTS)

CUs_gdb <- st_read(paste0(wd_geo_cus,"PSF_CUs_Master.gdb")) %>%
  st_transform(crs = 4269)

# remove non-used region values
cond <- CUs_gdb$region_name %in% regions_shp$Region
CUs_gdb <- CUs_gdb[cond,]

head(CUs_gdb)
unique(CUs_gdb$species_name)
unique(CUs_gdb$region_name)

# CUs_gdb_full <- CUs_gdb      
CUs_gdb <- st_simplify(x = CUs_gdb, dTolerance = .002) # to reduce computation time

# plot(st_geometry(CUs_gdb_full[1,]))
# plot(st_geometry(CUs_gdb[1,]), add = F)
```

# Initial modifications

```{r, include=FALSE}

#'* Remove rows for Atlantic, Steelhead, and Kokanee *

row_remove <- all_areas_nuseds$SPECIES %in% c("Steelhead","Atlantic","Kokanee")
nb_rows_NUSEDS <- sum(row_remove)
nb_rows_NUSEDS # 980 967 941

nb_POP_ID_NUSEDS <- length(unique(all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID[row_remove]))
nb_POP_ID_NUSEDS # 133 133

all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!row_remove,]

# all_areas_nuseds <- dplyr::filter(all_areas_nuseds, 
#                                   !SPECIES %in% c("Steelhead","Atlantic","Kokanee"))

row_remove <- !conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES_QUALIFIED %in% c("CK","CO","PKE","PKO","CM","SEL","SER")
nb_rows_CUSS <- sum(row_remove)
nb_POP_ID_CUSS <- length(unique(conservation_unit_census_sites$POP_ID[row_remove]))

conservation_unit_census_sites <- conservation_unit_census_sites[!row_remove,]

# conservation_unit_census_sites <- dplyr::filter(conservation_unit_census_sites, 
#                                                 SPECIES_QUALIFIED %in% sp_salmon_names_acro_df$acronym)
```

We remove from **NUSEDS** and **CUSS** data related to steelhead, Atlantic salmon and Kokanee salmon, corresponding to `r nb_rows_NUSEDS` rows and `r nb_POP_ID_NUSEDS` populations (`POP_ID`) in **NUSEDS** and `r nb_rows_CUSS` in **CUSS**.

```{r, include=FALSE}

#'* Rename the year column *
colnames(all_areas_nuseds)[colnames(all_areas_nuseds) == "ANALYSIS_YR"] <- "Year"

#'* Add the field SPECIES to CUSS *
conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES <- NA
species <- c("Coho","Chinook","Pink","Pink","Chum","Sockeye","Sockeye")
sp_acronym_q <- c("CO","CK","PKE","PKO","CM","SEL","SER")
for(spq in sp_acronym_q){
  # spq <- sp_acronym_q[1]
  condition <- conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES_QUALIFIED == spq
  sp_Here <- unique(species[spq == sp_acronym_q])
  conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES[condition] <- sp_Here
}
unique(conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES)


#'* Create the field "species_acronym_ncc"to NUSEDS *
#' i.e., CN, SX instead of CK and SER or SEL in the SPECIES_QUALIFIED.
#' There is no information about sockeye river vs. lake in NUSEDS, 
#' contrary to in conservation_unit_census_sites. We consequently have to create
#' the field species_acronym_ncc in both dataset to be able to merge them after.
#' (Note that there is information about the rearing locations the fieldsWATERBODY,
#' GAZETTED_NAME, LOCAL_NAME_1, LOCAL_NAME_2, POPULATION).
all_areas_nuseds$species_acronym_ncc <- conservation_unit_census_sites$species_acronym_ncc <- NA
species <- c("Coho","Chinook","Pink","Chum","Sockeye")
species_acronym_ncc <- c("CO","CN","PK","CM","SX")
for(sp in species){
  # sp <- species[3]
  sp_acroHere <- species_acronym_ncc[sp == species]
  condition <- all_areas_nuseds$SPECIES == sp
  all_areas_nuseds$species_acronym_ncc[condition] <- sp_acroHere
  condition <- conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES == sp
  conservation_unit_census_sites$species_acronym_ncc[condition] <- sp_acroHere
  
  # For Pink, add E and O for Even and Odd, respectively
  if(sp == "Pink"){
    all_areas_nuseds$species_acronym_ncc[all_areas_nuseds$SPECIES == sp & 
                                           all_areas_nuseds$Year %% 2 == 0] <- "PKE"
    all_areas_nuseds$species_acronym_ncc[all_areas_nuseds$SPECIES == sp & 
                                           all_areas_nuseds$Year %% 2 != 0] <- "PKO"
    
    condition <- conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES_QUALIFIED == "PKE"
    conservation_unit_census_sites$species_acronym_ncc[condition] <- "PKE"
    
    condition <- conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES_QUALIFIED == "PKO"
    conservation_unit_census_sites$species_acronym_ncc[condition] <- "PKO"
  }
}
unique(all_areas_nuseds$species_acronym_ncc)
unique(conservation_unit_census_sites$species_acronym_ncc)

```

We create the column `IndexId`, which the the combination of the species acronym (i.e., CO, CM, CN, PKO, PKE, SX) and `POP_ID`:

```{r,echo=FALSE}
#' * Add the field IndexId = species_acronym_ncc + POP_ID *
all_areas_nuseds$IndexId <- paste(all_areas_nuseds$species_acronym_ncc,
                                  all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID,sep = "_")

conservation_unit_census_sites$IndexId <- paste(conservation_unit_census_sites$species_acronym_ncc,
                                                conservation_unit_census_sites$POP_ID,sep = "_")

head(unique(conservation_unit_census_sites$IndexId))
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
#'* Add the field StatArea to NUSEDS *  TO DELTE
#' Old code, not sure how important this is.
# all_areas_nuseds$StatArea <- Convert2StatArea(all_areas_nuseds$AREA)
# Make corrections for populations with discrepancies in area assignments #
# These errors become apparent when merging data frames later on #
# all_areas_nuseds[all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CO_46240",]$StatArea <- "29"    # vs. "29J" "29K"
# all_areas_nuseds[all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "PKO_51094",]$StatArea <- "12"  # BSC: there is one ""
# all_areas_nuseds[all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "SX_45495",]$StatArea <- "120"  # BSC: already "120"
```

```{r, include=F}

# The filed Returns is not used but we keep the code just in case.

#'* Determine "Returns" (i.e. number fish) in NUSEDS (NOT USED - TO REMOVED?) *
#' "Return" will be the column that contains the final fish count. Priority of the
#' fields to population Returns:
#'1) NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS, if not available:
#'2) sum of 
#'  - NATURAL_SPAWNERS_TOTAL
#'  - ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS (or TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS if not available)
#'  - OTHER_REMOVALS
#'3) TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER

# Add "Return" which will contain the final number of fish
all_areas_nuseds$Returns <- all_areas_nuseds$NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS         # All salmon that have reached maturity, excluding jacks (jacks are salmon that have matured at an early age).
all_areas_nuseds$Source <- "NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS"                        # this field will change below depending on data availability and origin
NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS)
all_areas_nuseds$Source[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any] <- NA

#' Define the sum of:
#' - "Spawner" = NATURAL_SPAWNERS_TOTAL +
#' - "Broodstock" = ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS (or TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS if not available) + 
#' - "Removals" = OTHER_REMOVALS

# Spawners:
all_areas_nuseds$Spawners <- all_areas_nuseds$NATURAL_SPAWNERS_TOTAL
spawners_any <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$Spawners)
all_areas_nuseds$SpawnersSource[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any & spawners_any] <- "NATURAL_SPAWNERS_TOTAL"

# Broodstock:
all_areas_nuseds$Broodstock <- all_areas_nuseds$ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS
ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS_any <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS)
TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS_any <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS)
toReplace <- !ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS_any & TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS_any
all_areas_nuseds$Broodstock[toReplace] <- all_areas_nuseds$TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS[toReplace]
all_areas_nuseds$BroodstockSource[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any & ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS_any] <- 'ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS'
all_areas_nuseds$BroodstockSource[toReplace] <- 'TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS'

# Removals:
all_areas_nuseds$Removals <- all_areas_nuseds$OTHER_REMOVALS
OTHER_REMOVALS_any <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$OTHER_REMOVALS)
all_areas_nuseds$RemovalsSource[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any & OTHER_REMOVALS_any] <- "OTHER_REMOVALS"

# Calculate Returns when !NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any as the sum of what other 
# sources of data is available:
all_areas_nuseds$Returns[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any] <- apply(
  X = all_areas_nuseds[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any, c("Spawners","Broodstock","Removals")],
  MARGIN = 1, 
  FUN = sum, 
  na.rm = TRUE
)

all_areas_nuseds$Source[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any] <- apply(
  X = all_areas_nuseds[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any,c("SpawnersSource","BroodstockSource","RemovalsSource")], 
  MARGIN = 1, 
  FUN = function(x){
    paste(na.omit(x),collapse=" + ")}
)

# Set as NA rather than zero if no info in any column (the na.rm = T above produced)
# 0s when only NAs were available).
allNAs <- apply(
  X = all_areas_nuseds[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any, c("Spawners","Broodstock","Removals")],
  MARGIN = 1, 
  FUN = function(x){all(is.na(x))}
) 
all_areas_nuseds$Returns[!NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS_any][allNAs] <- NA

# Use TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER if we still don't have a value
returns_any <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$Returns)
TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER_any <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER)
toReplace <- !returns_any & TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER_any
all_areas_nuseds$Returns[toReplace] <- all_areas_nuseds$TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER[toReplace]
all_areas_nuseds$Source[toReplace] <- "TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER"
```

```{r, include=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#'* Determine "MAX_ESTIMATE" in NUSEDS *

#' MAX_ESTIMATE is the maximum estimate of all these fields:
var_in_MAX_ESTIMATE <- c("NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS", 
                         "NATURAL_JACK_SPAWNERS", 
                         "NATURAL_SPAWNERS_TOTAL", 
                         "ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS", 
                         "JACK_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS",
                         "TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS",    
                         "OTHER_REMOVALS", 
                         "TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER")

all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE <- apply(all_areas_nuseds[,var_in_MAX_ESTIMATE], 1,
                                       max, na.rm = TRUE)

# Replace infinite values by NA
all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE[is.infinite(all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE)] <- NA
```

We create the column `MAX_ESTIMATE` which is the maximum value of the fish count columns:

```{r,echo=FALSE}
var_in_MAX_ESTIMATE
```

# Fixes on NUSEDS and CUSS

```{r, include=F}
all_areas_nuseds_all <- all_areas_nuseds
nrow(all_areas_nuseds_all) # 418064 415230 412492

conservation_unit_census_sites_all <- conservation_unit_census_sites
nrow(conservation_unit_census_sites_all) # 7149 7145
```

```{r, include=FALSE}

# CHECKS

# Number of locations:
GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS <- unique(all_areas_nuseds_all[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])
nrow(GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS) # 11591 11568

GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites_all[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])
nrow(GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS) # 7149 7145

# Check the populations (unique IndexId & GFE_ID combo) in NUSEDS and not in CUCS and vice versa
GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS_collapse <- apply(GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS,1, paste, collapse = "_")
GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS_collapse <- gsub(" ","",GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS_collapse)

GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS_collapse <- apply(GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS,1, paste, collapse = "_")
GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS_collapse <- gsub(" ","",GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS_collapse)

sum(!GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS_collapse %in% GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS_collapse) # 4447 4428
sum(!GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS_collapse %in% GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS_collapse) # 5 5

```

The major issue in NuSEDS is the discrepancy between the two datasets. Normally, there should be the same population references (i.e., unique `IndexId`/`POP_ID` & `GFE_ID` combinations). But there are `r nrow(GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS)` unique population references in **NUSEDS** and only `r nrow(GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS)` in **CUSS**. Additionally, there are `r sum(!GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS_collapse %in% GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS_collapse)` references in **NUSEDS** that are not in **CUSS** and `r sum(!GFE_ID_IndexId_CUSS_collapse %in% GFE_ID_IndexId_NUSEDS_collapse)` that are in **CUSS** but not in **NUSEDS**. This section consists in solving these discrepancies so that the same population references are present in both datasets.

## Duplicated rows

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cols_NuSEDS <- c("SPECIES","POP_ID","GFE_ID","Year",
                 "NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS","NATURAL_JACK_SPAWNERS",
                 "NATURAL_SPAWNERS_TOTAL","ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS",
                 "JACK_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS","TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS",
                 "OTHER_REMOVALS","TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER")

cols_NuSEDS <- c(cols_NuSEDS,"ENUMERATION_METHODS","ESTIMATE_CLASSIFICATION")
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
#' Remove duplicated rows:
# dupli <- all_areas_nuseds %>%
#   dplyr::group_by(SPECIES, POP_ID, GFE_ID, ANALYSIS_YR,
#                   NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS,NATURAL_JACK_SPAWNERS,        
#                   NATURAL_SPAWNERS_TOTAL, ADULT_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS, 
#                   JACK_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS, TOTAL_BROODSTOCK_REMOVALS,    
#                   OTHER_REMOVALS, TOTAL_RETURN_TO_RIVER) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(n = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
#   dplyr::filter(n > 1L)
# 
# dupli # one

dupli <- duplicated(all_areas_nuseds[,cols_NuSEDS])
```

There are `r sum(dupli)` duplicated rows in **NUSEDS** when considering the following fields:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cols_NuSEDS
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
show <- all_areas_nuseds[dupli,c("SPECIES","POP_ID","GFE_ID","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE")] |>
  arrange(POP_ID,Year)

show
```

We assume these are real duplicated rows in **NUSEDS** so we remove them.

```{r, include=FALSE}
toRemove_r <- which(dupli)
all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[-toRemove_r,]
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
dupli <- duplicated(conservation_unit_census_sites)
```

For **CUSS**, `r sum(dupli)` duplicated rows are found:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
toRemove_r <- which(dupli)
show <- conservation_unit_census_sites[toRemove_r,]
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

These rows (if any) are removed as well.

```{r, include=FALSE}
conservation_unit_census_sites[-toRemove_r,]
```

## Conflictual counts in NUSEDS

```{r, include=FALSE}
# check if there are years for which there are more than one MAX_ESTIMATE value
# for a given population:
show <- unique(all_areas_nuseds[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE","ESTIMATE_METHOD","ESTIMATE_CLASSIFICATION")])

show$pop_yr <- paste(show$IndexId,show$GFE_ID,show$Year,sep = "-")

dupli <- duplicated(show$pop_yr)
pop_yr_dupli <- show$pop_yr[which(dupli)]
```

There are `r length(pop_yr_dupli)` instances where multiple `MAX_ESTIMATE` values are available in a same `Year` for a given population:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cond <- show$pop_yr %in% pop_yr_dupli
show <- show[cond,]
rownames(show) <- NULL

show[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE","ESTIMATE_METHOD","ESTIMATE_CLASSIFICATION")]
```

For `CN_45164`, we keep the observation with the largest count because the method associated is better (TYPE 1 vs. TYPE 4).

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cond_remove <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_45164" & 
  all_areas_nuseds$Year == 2023 & 
  all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2224 & 
  all_areas_nuseds$ESTIMATE_METHOD == "Peak Live + Dead"

all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!cond_remove,]
```

For `CN_46447`, there are different `CREATED_DTT` and `UPDATED_DTT` for the two entries:

```{r, echo = FALSE}
cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_46447" & 
  all_areas_nuseds$Year == 2016 & 
  all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 241

show <- all_areas_nuseds[cond,c(c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year","NATURAL_ADULT_SPAWNERS",
                          "ESTIMATE_METHOD",
                          "CREATED_DTT","UPDATED_DTT"))]
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

We keep the observation with the most recent entry: the one with `UPDATED_DTT` = 26-MAR-25.

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cond_remove <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_46447" & 
  all_areas_nuseds$Year == 2016 & 
  all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 241 & 
  all_areas_nuseds$CREATED_DTT == "06-AUG-21"

all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!cond_remove,]
```

```{r,echo=FALSE}

# print("Rows removed from NUSEDS:")

# PREVIOUS CODE:
# because there are only a couple cases, it is better to solve those individaully
# than automatically like it is done below

# For each of these cases, we keep the observation with the maximum `MAX_ESTIMATE` value and remove the other(s) from **NUSEDS**:

# for(x in pop_yr_dupli){
#   # x <- pop_yr_dupli[1]
#   val <- strsplit(x = x, split = "-")[[1]]
#   IndexId <- val[1]
#   GFE_ID <- val[2]
#   Year <- val[3]
#   
#   cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == IndexId &
#     all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == GFE_ID &
#     all_areas_nuseds$Year == Year
#   
#   # which one to keep
#   cond_show <- show$IndexId == IndexId &
#     show$GFE_ID == GFE_ID &
#     show$Year == Year
#   
#   cond_keep <- show$MAX_ESTIMATE[cond_show] == max(show$MAX_ESTIMATE[cond_show])
#   MAX_ESTIMATE_keep <-  show$MAX_ESTIMATE[cond_keep]
#   
#   #
#   cond_nuseds_remove <- cond_nuseds & 
#     all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE != MAX_ESTIMATE_keep &
#     !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE)
#   
#   cond_nuseds_keep <- cond_nuseds & 
#     all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE == MAX_ESTIMATE_keep &
#     !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE)
#   
#   
#   to_print_r <- all_areas_nuseds[cond_nuseds_remove,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE","ESTIMATE_CLASSIFICATION","ESTIMATE_METHOD")]
#   to_print_r$remove <- T
#   to_print_k <- all_areas_nuseds[cond_nuseds_keep,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE","ESTIMATE_CLASSIFICATION","ESTIMATE_METHOD")]
#   to_print_k$remove <- F
#   to_print <- rbind(to_print_r,to_print_k)
#   rownames(to_print) <- NULL
#   print(to_print)
#   
#   # remove from NUSEDS
#   all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!cond_nuseds_remove,]
# }

```

## Location without Adaptive Zone data in CUSS 

There is one instance of a location (`GFE_ID`) that does not have values for `FAZ_ACRO`,`MAZ_ACRO`,`JAZ_ACRO`:

```{r,echo=F}
GFE_IDs <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites_all$GFE_ID)

# Are there GFE_IDs associated to multiple zones: --> just one and it is a mistake
for(val in GFE_IDs){
  cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == val
  out <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[cond,c("CENSUS_SITE","GFE_ID","FAZ_ACRO","MAZ_ACRO","JAZ_ACRO")])
  
  cond <- length(unique(out$FAZ_ACRO)) > 1 |  length(unique(out$MAZ_ACRO)) > 1 | length(unique(out$JAZ_ACRO)) > 1
  
  if(cond){
    rownames(out) <- NULL
    print(out)
  }
}
# CENSUS_SITE GFE_ID FAZ_ACRO MAZ_ACRO JAZ_ACRO
# 50   PUNTLEDGE RIVER   1156      EVI     GStr EVI+GStr
# 4640 PUNTLEDGE RIVER   1156       
```

We edit **CUSS** accordingly.

```{r, include=FALSE}
cond_toFix <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == 1156 & conservation_unit_census_sites$FAZ_ACRO == ""
cond_toReplace <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == 1156 & conservation_unit_census_sites$FAZ_ACRO != ""

conservation_unit_census_sites$FAZ_ACRO[cond_toFix] <- conservation_unit_census_sites$FAZ_ACRO[cond_toReplace] |> unique()
conservation_unit_census_sites$MAZ_ACRO[cond_toFix] <- conservation_unit_census_sites$MAZ_ACRO[cond_toReplace] |> unique()
conservation_unit_census_sites$JAZ_ACRO[cond_toFix] <- conservation_unit_census_sites$JAZ_ACRO[cond_toReplace] |> unique()
```


## Missing coordinates in CUSS


```{r, include=FALSE}
# Add the GFE_IDs in CUSS that are missing
cond_cuss <- is.na(conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT)
GFE_IDs_here_cuss <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID[cond_cuss])

length(GFE_IDs_here_cuss) # 9

i <- 1
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # NICOLA RIVER (DAM)
# at the dam
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -120.665873
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 50.163045 

i <- i + 1 
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # NICOLA RIVER (DOT)
# From ChatGPT:
#' “NICOLA RIVER (DOT)” in NuSEDS refers to a salmon census site on the Nicola 
#' River near a Department of Transportation–maintained highway crossing — most 
#' likely the Highway 8 bridge near Merritt / Lower Nicola area, where visual 
#' counts or monitoring infrastructure have been historically used.
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -121.315659
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 50.424454 

i <- i + 1
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # BIRCH ISLAND CHANNEL
# cf. map p. 31 of Arbeider et al. 2020. Interior Fraser Coho Salmon Recovery Potential Assessment
# https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40888721.pdf
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -119.889197
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 51.598602

i <- i + 1
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # BLUE RIVER - UPPER
# the GFE_ID is in the DFO_All_Streams_Segments, but coordinates are missing there too
# cond <- DFO_All_Streams_Segments$NME == "BLUE RIVER - UPPER"
# DFO_All_Streams_Segments[cond,]
# So we define them manually
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -119.289006
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.112252

i <- i + 1 # 5
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # MIDDLE NORTH THOMPSON RIVER MAINSTEM
# cf. map p. 31 of Arbeider et al. 2020. Interior Fraser Coho Salmon Recovery Potential Assessment
# https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40888721.pdf
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -119.701668
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 51.593091

i <- i + 1
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # PIG CHANNEL COMBINED
# cf. map p. 31 of Arbeider et al. 2020. Interior Fraser Coho Salmon Recovery Potential Assessment
# https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40888721.pdf
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- 119.810014
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 51.579465

i <- i + 1
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # UPPER NORTH THOMPSON RIVER MAINSTEM
# cf. map p. 31 of Arbeider et al. 2020. Interior Fraser Coho Salmon Recovery Potential Assessment
# https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40888721.pdf
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -119.177796
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.345236

i <- i + 1
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # UPPER NORTH THOMPSON RIVER-MILEDGE CONFLUENCE
# cf. map p. 31 of Arbeider et al. 2020. Interior Fraser Coho Salmon Recovery Potential Assessment
# https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40888721.pdf
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -119.173514
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.281312

i <- i + 1
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here_cuss[i]
conservation_unit_census_sites$CENSUS_SITE[cond] |> unique() # CHILCOTIN RIVER - UPPER
# It is in DFO_hatchery:
# condx <- DFO_hatchery$STOCK_GFE_NAME == "CHILCOTIN RIVER (UPPER)" & !is.na(DFO_hatchery$STOCK_GFE_NAME)
# DFO_hatchery$STOCK_LONGITUDE[condx] |> unique()
# DFO_hatchery$STOCK_LATITUDE[condx] |> unique()
# there are two different coordinates for this GFE_ID
# We pick the 1st one, which points to the Chilcotin Lake, the second one
# points to above the lake
# 52.339295 -124.028396 
# 52.362222 -124.009489
conservation_unit_census_sites$X_LONGT[cond] <- -124.028396
conservation_unit_census_sites$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.339295

conservation_unit_census_sites$coordinates_changed[cond_cuss] <- T
```

There are `r sum(cond_cuss)` locations without coordinates in any of the source files and for which we have to define coordinates manually (cf., code to see the sources when available):

```{r, echo=FALSE}
show <- conservation_unit_census_sites[cond_cuss,c("CENSUS_SITE","GFE_ID","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")]
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

## Remove the IndexId & GFE_ID time series in NUSEDS with only NAs and/or 0s

We remove these time series to reduce the amount of cleaning to do after. There is the option to remove series with (1) only NAs and 0s or (2) only NAs.

Decision made:

```{r,echo=FALSE}
if(remove_timeSeries_zeros_too){
  print("Time series uniqually made of both NAs and 0s are removed.")
}else{
  print("Time series uniqually made of NAs are removed (those also containing 0s are kept).")
}
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
nrow_before <- nrow(all_areas_nuseds)

# If all_areas_nuseds without NAs and 0s already produced; to save time
if(import_file_locally_TEMPORARY){
  
  if(remove_timeSeries_zeros_too){
      all_areas_nuseds <- read.csv(paste0(wd_data,"/all_areas_nuseds_noNA0s_TEMP.csv"),
                                   header = T)
  }else{
      all_areas_nuseds <- read.csv(paste0(wd_data,"/all_areas_nuseds_noNAs_TEMP.csv"),
                                   header = T)
  }
  
}else{
  # Use package parallel To optimize the (take up to 20 minutes otherwise)
  # detectCores()
  # detectCores(logical = FALSE)
  cores_nb <- 10
  
  # Note: Passing remove_timeSeries_zeros_too as an argument in remove_series_nodata_nuseds_parallel_fun(zeros_too = ) does not work
  if(remove_timeSeries_zeros_too){
      all_areas_nuseds <- remove_series_nodata_nuseds_parallel_fun(all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                                   zeros_too = T, # to consider time series with only NAs and/or 0s
                                                                   cores_nb = cores_nb)
      
      write.csv(all_areas_nuseds,paste0(wd_data,"/all_areas_nuseds_noNA0s_TEMP.csv"),
                row.names = F)
        
  }else{
      all_areas_nuseds <- remove_series_nodata_nuseds_parallel_fun(all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                                   zeros_too = F, # only consider time series with only NAs
                                                                   cores_nb = cores_nb)
      
      write.csv(all_areas_nuseds,paste0(wd_data,"/all_areas_nuseds_noNAs_TEMP.csv"),
                row.names = F)
  }
}

nrow(all_areas_nuseds) 
# 315898 newer
# 313285 (series with NAs removed) 312331 (series with NAs and/or 0s removed) 309647

nrow_after <- nrow(all_areas_nuseds)
```

This procedure removes `r nrow_before - nrow_after` rows in **NUSEDS**, corresponding to `r round((nrow_before - nrow_after)/nrow_before*100,1)`% of the original dataset.

```{r, include = F}
# Record the series that were removed and why:
IndexId_GFE_ID_all <- unique(all_areas_nuseds_all[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])
nrow(IndexId_GFE_ID_all) # 11591 11568 11553

IndexId_GFE_ID <- unique(all_areas_nuseds[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])
nrow(IndexId_GFE_ID) # 7166 7084 7062

IndexId_GFE_ID_all <- paste(IndexId_GFE_ID_all$IndexId,IndexId_GFE_ID_all$GFE_ID,sep = "&")
IndexId_GFE_ID <- paste(IndexId_GFE_ID$IndexId,IndexId_GFE_ID$GFE_ID,sep = "&")

IndexId_GFE_ID_removed_c <- IndexId_GFE_ID_all[! IndexId_GFE_ID_all %in% IndexId_GFE_ID]
length(IndexId_GFE_ID_removed_c) # 4425 4484 4491

IndexId_GFE_ID_removed <- data.frame(IndexId = rep(NA,length(IndexId_GFE_ID_removed_c)),
                                     GFE_ID = rep(NA,length(IndexId_GFE_ID_removed_c)))

IndexId_GFE_ID_removed$IndexId <- sapply(X = IndexId_GFE_ID_removed_c, FUN = function(s){
  # s <- IndexId_GFE_ID_removed[1]
  return(strsplit(x = s, split = "&")[[1]][1])
  })

IndexId_GFE_ID_removed$GFE_ID <- sapply(X = IndexId_GFE_ID_removed_c, FUN = function(s){
  # s <- IndexId_GFE_ID_removed[1]
  return(strsplit(x = s, split = "&")[[1]][2])
})


if(remove_timeSeries_zeros_too){
  comment <- "Only NAs and/or 0s for MAX_ESTIMATE"
}else{
  comment <- "Only NAs for MAX_ESTIMATE"
}

removed_all <- IndexId_GFE_ID_removed
removed_all$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
removed_all$comment <- comment

# check
# i <- 3243
# plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = removed_all$IndexId[i],
#                         GFE_IDs = removed_all$GFE_ID[i],
#                         all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds_all)
nrow(removed_all) # 4425 4484 4491
```

The data removed concerns `r nrow(removed_all)` time series, which are referenced in `removed_all`:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
head(removed_all)
```

Note that we do not remove the references of these series (i.e., their `IndexId` & `GFE_ID` association) in **CUSS** in order to not loose information.

## Fix populations in NUSEDS that are not in CUSS

The goal is to look for each `IndexId/POP_ID` & `GFE_ID` series in **NUSEDS** that are not in **CUSS** and to proceed as follow:

1) The series with a `IndexId/POP_ID` associate with a `FULL_CU_IN` (field added in **NUSEDS** since 2025-11-03) are added to **CUSS**. The step might require to add a new `IndexId/POP_ID` and/or `GFE_ID` to **CUSS**.

2) The missing `FULL_CU_IN` (and consequently `CU_NAME` and other related fields) of the remaining series is found by intersecting the coordinates (`X_LONGT` and `Y_LAT`) associated with the `GFE_ID` with the PSE's CUs' shape files. Several `GFE_ID` are missing coordinates so we look in the DFO stream file and the DFO hatchery file as alternative sources. Once associated to a CU, these series are added to **CUSS**.

```{r, include=FALSE}
series_nuseds <- paste(all_areas_nuseds$IndexId,
                       all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID, sep = "&")
series_nuseds <- unique(series_nuseds)
length(series_nuseds) # 7166 7082 7060

series_cuss <- paste(conservation_unit_census_sites$IndexId,
                     conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID, sep = "&")
series_cuss <- unique(series_cuss)
length(series_cuss) # 7149 6894 6889

#' Check the number of series referemces in CUSS not in all_areas_nuseds.
#' To deal with later
series_cuss[! series_cuss %in% series_nuseds] |> length()
# 247

# Number of series in NUSEDS not in CUSS:
series_Nuseds_noCuss <- series_nuseds[! series_nuseds %in% series_cuss]
series_Nuseds_noCuss <- series_Nuseds_noCuss[order(series_Nuseds_noCuss)]
n <- length(series_Nuseds_noCuss) # 264 242 188 171
n


#' Create trackRecord_nuseds
iids <- sapply(X = series_Nuseds_noCuss,FUN = function(s){
  return(strsplit(s,"&")[[1]][1])
})

gfeids <- sapply(X = series_Nuseds_noCuss,FUN = function(s){
  return(strsplit(s,"&")[[1]][2])
})

trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss <- data.frame(IndexId = iids,
                                 GFE_ID = gfeids,
                                 FULL_CU_IN = NA)

rownames(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss) <- NULL

# NUSEDS now have FULL_CU_IN (as of update from 2025-11-03)
# but there is are lots of missing values:
# sapply(unique(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId), function(x){
#   cond <- all_areas_nuseds_all$IndexId == x
#   return(unique(all_areas_nuseds_all$FULL_CU_IN[cond]))
# })

# So we also look in CUSS
for(iid in unique(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId)){
  cond <- all_areas_nuseds_all$IndexId == iid
  FULL_CU_IN <- unique(all_areas_nuseds_all$FULL_CU_IN[cond])
  
  if(is.na(FULL_CU_IN) | FULL_CU_IN == ""){
    cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites_all$IndexId == iid
    FULL_CU_IN <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites_all$FULL_CU_IN[cond])
  }
  
  if(length(FULL_CU_IN) == 0){
    FULL_CU_IN <- ""
  }else if(is.na(FULL_CU_IN) | FULL_CU_IN == ""){
    FULL_CU_IN <- ""
  }
  
  cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId == iid
  trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$FULL_CU_IN[cond] <- FULL_CU_IN
}
```

There are `r nrow(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss)` `IndexId` & `GFE_ID` time series in **NUSEDS** whose references are not present in **CUSS**, among which `r sum(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$FULL_CU_IN != "")` already have a `FULL_CU_IN`.

```{r, include=FALSE}

trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY <- sapply(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID,function(gfeid){
  cond <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == gfeid
  return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY[cond]))
})

# Find coordinates GFE_ID
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT <- NA
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$coordinates_from <- NA

for(gfeid in unique(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID)){
  
  coordinates_from <- NA
  X_LONGT <- NA
  Y_LAT <- NA
  
  cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites_all$GFE_ID == gfeid
  
  X_LONGT <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites_all$X_LONGT[cond])
  Y_LAT <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites_all$Y_LAT[cond])
  
  if(length(X_LONGT) == 0){
    X_LONGT <- Y_LAT <- NA
  }
  
  if(!is.na(X_LONGT)){
    coordinates_from <- "CUSS"
    
  }else{
    # find the WATERBODY
    cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == gfeid
    WATERBODY <- unique(all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY[cond_nuseds])
    # print(paste("Need coordinates for: ",WATERBODY," - ",gfeid,sep = ""))
    
    # Check in DFO_All_Streams_Segments
    cond <- DFO_All_Streams_Segments$ID == gfeid
    X_LONGT <- unique(DFO_All_Streams_Segments$X_LONGT[cond])
    Y_LAT <- unique(DFO_All_Streams_Segments$Y_LAT[cond])
    
    if(length(X_LONGT) == 0){
      X_LONGT <- Y_LAT <- NA
    }
    
    if(!is.na(X_LONGT)){
      coordinates_from <- "DFO_All_Streams_Segments"
      
    }else{
      
      # Check in DFO_hatchery
      cond <- DFO_hatchery$REL_GFE_ID == gfeid & !is.na(DFO_hatchery$REL_GFE_ID)
      
      if(any(cond)){
        X_LONGT <- unique(DFO_hatchery$REL_LONGITUDE[cond])
        Y_LAT <- unique(DFO_hatchery$REL_LATITUDE[cond])
        
      }else{
        cond <- DFO_hatchery$STOCK_GFE_ID == gfeid & !is.na(DFO_hatchery$STOCK_GFE_ID)
        X_LONGT <- unique(DFO_hatchery$REL_LONGITUDE[cond])
        Y_LAT <- unique(DFO_hatchery$REL_LATITUDE[cond])
      }
      
      if(length(X_LONGT) == 0){
        X_LONGT <- Y_LAT <- NA
      }
      
      if(!is.na(X_LONGT)){
        coordinates_from <- "DFO_hatchery"
        
      }else{
        coordinates_from <- "Manually defined"
      }
    }
  }
  
  cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == gfeid
  trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- Y_LAT
  trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- X_LONGT
  trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$coordinates_from[cond] <- coordinates_from
}

t <- table(unique(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss[,c("GFE_ID","coordinates_from")])$coordinates_from)

t
```

Among the `r length(unique(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID))` locations (`GFE_ID`) associated to these series, `r t["CUSS"]` are present with coordinates in **CUSS**, `r t["DFO_All_Streams_Segments"]` had to be found in the DFO file of the stream locations, `r t["DFO_hatchery"]` were found in the DFO hatchery file and `r t["Manually defined"]` were not found anywhere. In the next section, we attribute coordinates to the latter manually.

### GFE_ID with missing X_LONGT and Y_LAT

There are `r t["Manually defined"]` locations with coordinates we manually defined using Google Maps or any resource we could find.

There are two *Clearwater Creek* `WATERBODY` associated to *Phillips River (Campbell River Area)* `POPULATION` in **NUSEDS**:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# cond <- all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY %in% c("CLEARWATER CREEK","Clearwater Creek")
cond <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID %in% c(2618,446068199)
show <- unique(all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("WATERBODY","GFE_ID")])
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

None of the two `GFE_ID` are in **CUSS**

```{r}
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID %in% c(2618,446068199)
show <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[cond,c("CENSUS_SITE","GFE_ID","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")])
show <- as.data.frame(show)
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

One of them is in the **DFO file of the stream locations**:

```{r}
cond <- DFO_All_Streams_Segments$ID %in% c(2618,446068199)
show <- unique(DFO_All_Streams_Segments[cond,c("NME","ID","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")])
show <- as.data.frame(show)
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

By comparing all the time series having the same `indexId/POP_ID` with those two `GFE_ID`, we can see all of them can be merged: 

```{r, echo=FALSE}
GFE_IDs <- c(2618,446068199)
cond_GFE_ID <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID %in% GFE_IDs

par(mar = c(5,5,.5,.5))
for(iid in unique(all_areas_nuseds$IndexId[cond_GFE_ID])){
  # iid <- unique(all_areas_nuseds$IndexId[cond])[2]
  
  cond <- cond_GFE_ID & all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == iid
  
  if(nrow(unique(all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])) > 1){
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = rep(iid,2), 
                          GFE_IDs = GFE_IDs, 
                          all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
  
      cond <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID %in% GFE_IDs[1] & all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == iid
      w1 <- unique(all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY[cond])
      cond <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID %in% GFE_IDs[2] & all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == iid
      w2 <- unique(all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY[cond])
      legend("topright",c(w1,w2), bty = "n")
  }
}
```

We consequently assume that the two locations are the same and we merge the time time series by replacing the `GFE_ID` value without coordinates (`446068199`) by the one with coordinates (`2618`) in **NUSEDS**.

```{r, echo=FALSE}
GFE_IDs <- c(2618,446068199)
cond_GFE_ID <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID %in% GFE_IDs

for(iid in unique(all_areas_nuseds$IndexId[cond_GFE_ID])){
  
    cond <- cond_GFE_ID & all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == iid
  
  if(nrow(unique(all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])) > 1){
    
    print(paste0("Series ",iid," - ",446068199," merged to series ",iid," - ",2618," in NUSEDS"))
    
    removed <- data.frame(IndexId = iid,
                          GFE_ID = 446068199)
    removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
    removed$comment <- c("Series not in CUSS, merged to OTHER FOCAL series with same IndexId and GFE_ID = 2618")
    removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
    
    all_areas_nuseds <- fields_edit_NUSEDS_CUSS_fun(edit_NUSEDS = T, 
                                                    IndexId_focal = iid,
                                                    IndexId_alter = iid,
                                                    GFE_ID_focal = 446068199,
                                                    GFE_ID_alter = 2618,
                                                    all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                    conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites)
    print("")
    
    # remove the reference from trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss because we do not have to deal 
    # with it
    cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId == iid & trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == 446068199
    trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss[!cond,]
  }
}
```

There is a typo for Cariboo river:

```{r, echo=TRUE}
cond <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2467
show <- unique(all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("WATERBODY","GFE_ID","IndexId","POPULATION")])
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

 We correct it to `CARIBOO RIVER - LOWER` in **NUSEDS** to match we other notations of the same river:
 
```{r, echo=TRUE}
cond <- grepl("CARRIBOO",all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY)
all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY[cond] <- "CARIBOO RIVER - LOWER"

cond <- grepl("CARRIBOO",trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY)
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] <- "CARIBOO RIVER - LOWER"

cond <- grepl("CARIBOO",all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY)
show <- unique(all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("WATERBODY","GFE_ID")])
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```
 
```{r, include=FALSE}
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$coordinates_from == "Manually defined"
GFE_IDs_here <- unique(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID[cond])

i <- 1
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # STAMP RIVER BELOW FALLS
# point takien from Google Maps below the Falls
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -124.911882
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.327917

i <- i + 1 
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # SPROAT RIVER BELOW FALLS
# point taken below the falls
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -124.902346
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.290073

i <- i + 1 
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # RETURN CHANNEL CREEKS
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # "CM_45931"  "PKE_45930" "PKO_45930"
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CM_45931"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Return Channel Creeks (Bella Bella) Chum
# Coordinate of Return Channel
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -128.135232
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.276281

# i <- i + 1 
# cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
# trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # Clearwater Creek --> Cayenne Creek ? to check
# trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # "CM_50616"  "CN_50618"  "CO_50612"  "PKO_50614" "SX_50610" 
# cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CM_50616"
# unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Phillips River (Campbell River Area) Chum Run 1
# cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "SX_50610"
# unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Phillips River (Campbell River Area) Chum Run 1

i <- i + 1 # 4
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # KENNEDY LAKE BEACHES
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -125.580441
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.049133

i <- i + 1 # 5 
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # SILVERMERE LAKE
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -122.408753
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.174967

i <- i +1 # 6
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # CAMILOS CREEK
# https://geonames.nrcan.gc.ca/search-place-names/unique?id=JASOL
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -121.393056
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.386667

i <- i +1 # 7
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # HANSEN CREEK
# https://geonames.nrcan.gc.ca/search-place-names/unique?id=JALWQ
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -128.346389
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 50.778889

i <- i +1 # 8
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # COTTONWOOD RIVER - LOWER
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID[cond] |> unique() # 2464
#' to the mouth of Cottonwood river
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -122.610584
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 53.119965

i <- i + 1 # 9
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # CARIBOO RIVER - LOWER (was CARRIBOO RIVER (LOWER))
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # CN_46891
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_46891"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Cariboo River (Williams Lake Area) Chinook Run 1
# Coordinates other locations in CUSS:
# CARIBOO RIVER           52.66253574	-121.6758417
# CARIBOO RIVER - UPPER   52.82509818	-121.2771638
# we pick below the lake
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -121.444278
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.721027

i <- i + 1 # 10
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # SKEENA RIVER - NORTH
# we pick by Terrace
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -128.636866
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 54.501018

i <- i + 1  # 11
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # WIMINASIK LAKE SYSTEM
# we pick WIMINASIK lake
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -128.049384
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 56.489746

i <- i + 1 # 12
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # NICOLA RIVER - LOWER
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID[cond] |> unique() # 2461
# other similar locations
# NICOLA RIVER: 50.42767089	-121.3162004
# NICOLA RIVER - UPPER: 50.13744698	-120.709084
# we pick near Spius Creek (Hatchery)
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -121.021924
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 50.145281

i <- i + 1 # 13
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # SEMMIHAULT CREEK
# https://geonames.nrcan.gc.ca/search-place-names/unique?id=JBHMC
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -121.956111
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.153056

i <- i + 1 # 14
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # MCLELLAN CREEK
# MCLELLAN CREEK
# there are multiple of them...
# "McLellan creek is located 8 km east of townsite Bella Coola"
# in https://www.env.gov.bc.ca/cariboo/env_stewardship/wrp/reports/fhap/six_creeks-bellacoola/the6cr.html
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # CO_7793
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CO_7793"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Mclellan Creek (Bella Coola) Coho
# and it is not the ones there:
# https://geonames.nrcan.gc.ca/search-place-names/search?id=&snrc=&q=MCLELLAN+CREEK&category=O
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -126.619731
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.387830

i <- i + 1 # 15
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # GIBSON CREEK   TODO
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # CO_7828
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CO_7828"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Gibson Creek Coho
# Probably the one in the Sunshine Coast according to these surveys
# https://thescca.ca/current-campaigns/ch%E1%B8%B5welhp-gibson-creek-cgc/
# and
# https://geonames.nrcan.gc.ca/search-place-names/unique?id=JAKXZ
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <-  -123.499173
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.406058

i <- i + 1 # 16
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # COTTONWOOD CREEK
# there are many of them...
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # CO_7793
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CO_792"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Cottonwood Creek (Squamish) Coho Run 1
# so not those ones:
# https://geonames.nrcan.gc.ca/search-place-names/search?id=&snrc=&q=COTTONWOOD+CREEK&category=O
# selected the stream leading to Cottonwood Park in Squamish
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -123.145631
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.762523

i <- i + 1 # 17
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # FEE CREEK
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # CO_959
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CO_959"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Fee Creek (Squamish) Coho Run 1
# There is a stream coming down from Mount Fee to the Squamish river, here is the 
# mouth's coordinates:
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -123.342487
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 50.068778

i <- i + 1 # 18
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # Atlatzi+Clear Rivers
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # PKO_51094
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "PKO_51094"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Kingcome River (Alert Bay) Pink Run 1
#' Mouth of the stream going in the Atlatzi Reserve
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -126.119180
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 51.015775

i <- i + 1 # 19
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # OLSEN CREEK
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # SX_1874
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "SX_1874"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Olsen Creek (Coquitlam) Early Summer Sockeye Run 1
# in https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2017/mpo-dfo/Fs23-323-1-1997-eng.pdf
#  "Olsen Creek (which enters the Pitt River opposite Fish Hatchery Creek) on the Upper Pitt River"
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -122.631537
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.597168

i <- i + 1 # 20
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # CLAYOQUOT ARM BEACHES
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -125.591878
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 49.099033

i <- i + 1 
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # MOORE LAKE
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # SX_7805
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "SX_7805"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Moore Lake (Grenville Principe) Sockeye
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -129.501134
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 53.409530 

i <- i + 1 
cond <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == GFE_IDs_here[i]
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[cond] |> unique() # ATNARKO LAKES
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[cond] # SX_7818
cond_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "SX_7818"
unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond_nuseds]) # Atnarko Lakes Sockeye
# 'https://www.ccira.ca/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/AtnarkoSockeyRecoveryPlan-FullSizeRender-45.pdf
#' the five lakes:
#' - Elbow
#' - Rainbow
#' - Tenas      --> 52.163834, -125.713941
#' - Lonesome
#' - Stillwater
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[cond] <- -125.713941
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[cond] <- 52.163834
```

Here are the locations for which we manually defined coordinates:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
show <- unique(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss[,c("GFE_ID","WATERBODY","X_LONGT","Y_LAT","coordinates_from")])
show <- show[show$coordinates_from  == "Manually defined",c("GFE_ID","WATERBODY","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")]
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

### Find the CU of population without a FULL_CU_ID

```{r, echo=FALSE}
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$POPULATION <- sapply(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId,function(iid){
  cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == iid
  return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond]))
})

cond_noCU <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$FULL_CU_IN == ""
show <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss[cond_noCU,c("IndexId","POPULATION")] |> unique()

rownames(show) <- NULL

# check that none of those IndexId/POP_ID are in CUSS:
# sum(show %in% conservation_unit_census_sites$IndexId)
```

There are `r nrow(show)` `IndexId/POP_ID` not associated to a CU (and `r sum(cond_noCU)` time series):

```{r,echo=FALSE}
show
```

We identify the CU they belong to using the CUs' shape files of the PSE. Series with `X_LONGT` and `Y_LAT` values that intersects one unique CU layer of the same species are attributed the CU's `FULL_CU_IN`. When more than one CU layer is intersected, we use the information in `POPULATION` and `WATERBODY` to manually select the correct CU if possible.

```{r, include=FALSE}

#' Here we find the cuid and cu_name_pse to the time series without and without a 
#' FULL_CU_IN.


# check that all the FULL_CU_IN in NUSEDS are in CUSS:
# FULL_CU_IN_nuseds <- unique(all_areas_nuseds$FULL_CU_IN)
# FULL_CU_IN_nuseds <- FULL_CU_IN_nuseds[FULL_CU_IN_nuseds != ""]
# all(FULL_CU_IN_nuseds %in% unique(conservation_unit_census_sites$FULL_CU_IN)) # TRUE

trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$CU_NAME <- sapply(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$FULL_CU_IN,function(fci){
  if(fci == ""){
    out <- ""
  }else{
    cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites_all$FULL_CU_IN == fci
    out <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites_all$CU_NAME[cond])
  }
  return(out)
})

trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$species_name <- sapply(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId,function(fci){
  
  if(grepl("CM",fci)){
    species <- "Chum"
  }else if(grepl("CO",fci)){
    species <- "Coho"
  }else if(grepl("CN",fci)){
    species <- "Chinook"
  }else if(grepl("PK",fci)){
    species <- "Pink"
  }else if(grepl("SX",fci)){
    species <- "Sockeye"
  }
  return(species)
})

# trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss_copy1 <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss
# trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss_copy1

trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$cuid <- NA
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$cu_name_pse <- NA
trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$comment <- NA

for(r in 1:nrow(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss)){
  # r <- 1
  # r <- which(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY == "KENNEDY LAKE BEACHES" & trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId == "SX_52080")
  # r <- which(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID == 1194 & trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId == "CN_3334")
  # r <- which(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId == "CN_3333") only one row/GFE_ID
  # r <- 130 r <- 131 case with CO_7776 which(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId == "CO_7776")
  
  # print(paste0("*** r = ",r," ***"))
  
  FULL_CU_IN <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$FULL_CU_IN[r]
  CU_NAME <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$CU_NAME[r]
  species_name <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$species_name[r]
  IndexId <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r]
  
  comment <- NA
  
  if(FULL_CU_IN != ""){ # find the cuid and cu_name_pse from decoder
    
    cond <- FULL_CU_IN == conservationunits_decoder$cu_index & !is.na(conservationunits_decoder$cu_index)
    
    if(length(unique(conservationunits_decoder$cuid[cond])) == 0){
      
      if(species_name == "Pink"){ # need to remove odd and even from cu_name_dfo
        
        cu_name_dfo <- simplify_string_fun(conservationunits_decoder$cu_name_dfo)
        cu_name_dfo <- gsub("pinkoddyear","",cu_name_dfo)
        cu_name_dfo <- gsub("odd","",cu_name_dfo)
        cu_name_dfo <- gsub("even","",cu_name_dfo)
        
        if(grepl("PKE",trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r])){
          species_qualified <- "PKE"
        }else{
          species_qualified <- "PKO"
        }
        
        cond <- conservationunits_decoder$species_qualified == species_qualified &
          simplify_string_fun(CU_NAME) == cu_name_dfo
        
      }else{
        cond <- simplify_string_fun(CU_NAME) == simplify_string_fun(conservationunits_decoder$cu_name_dfo) & 
          conservationunits_decoder$species_name == species_name
      }
    }
    
    if(length(unique(conservationunits_decoder$cuid[cond])) == 1){
      
      trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$cuid[r] <- conservationunits_decoder$cuid[cond] |> unique()
      trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$cu_name_pse[r] <- conservationunits_decoder$cu_name_pse[cond] |> unique()
      comment <- "FULL_CU_IN already in CUSS (so it is the IndexId & GFE_ID that in not in CUSS)"
      
    }else{
      
      cond_SX_3302 <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r] == "SX_3302"
      #' POPULATION = Somass System Sockeye
      #' WATERBODY = GREAT CENTRAL LAKE
      #' FULL_CU_IN = SEL-13-xx
      #' CU_NAME = GREAT CENTRAL/SPROAT
      #' There are two choices:
      #' - West Vancouver Island         SER-10     SER Current
      #' -         Great Central      SEL-13-08     SEL Current 
      
      if(cond_SX_3302){
        comment <- "CUID not found: IndexId = SEL-13-xx and CU_NAME = GREAT CENTRAL/SPROAT"
        
      }else{
        print("Issue with FULL_CU_IN that is not in the decoder - BREAK")
        knitr::knit_exit()
        break
      }
    }
    
    # we use the shape files
  }else{
    
    GFE_ID <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID[r]
    Y_LAT <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$Y_LAT[r]
    X_LONGT <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$X_LONGT[r]
    
    cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId ==  trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r] & 
      all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID ==  trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID[r]
    
    POPULATION <- unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond])
    WATERBODY <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$WATERBODY[r]
    
    # plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r],
    #                         GFE_IDs = trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$GFE_ID[r],
    #                         all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
    # 
    # legend("bottomleft",paste0("i = ",r),bty = "n")
    
    # Find the region
    # https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75669453/r-check-to-see-if-coordinates-fall-inside-outside-of-a-shapefile-polygon
    point <- st_as_sf(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss[r,], 
                      coords = c("X_LONGT","Y_LAT"), crs = 4269)
    
    layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, regions_shp)
    
    if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
      layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, st_buffer(x = regions_shp, dist = .001))
    }
    
    if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
      layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, st_buffer(x = regions_shp, dist = .002))
    }
    
    if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
      print("Region layer not found - BREAK")
      knitr::knit_exit()
      break
    }
    
    # if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
    #   layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, st_buffer(x = regions_shp, dist = .01))
    # }
    # if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
    #   layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, st_buffer(x = regions_shp, dist = .05))
    # }
    # if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
    #   layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, st_buffer(x = regions_shp, dist = .1))
    # }
    # if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
    #   layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, st_buffer(x = regions_shp, dist = .2))
    # }
    # if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) == 0){ # no match, try to buffer
    #   layer_rg <- st_intersects(point, st_buffer(x = regions_shp, dist = .4))
    # }
    
    # CHECK
    # plot(st_geometry(regions_shp))
    # plot(st_geometry(regions_shp[layer_rg[[1]],]))
    # plot(st_geometry(point), add = T, col = "red", pch = 16, cex = 2)
    
    if(length(layer_rg[[1]]) > 1){
      print("More than one region - BREAK")
      knitr::knit_exit()
      break
      
    }else{
      layer_rg <- layer_rg[[1]]
      region <- regions_shp$Region[layer_rg]
      
      cond_rg_sp <- CUs_gdb$region_name == region & CUs_gdb$species_name == species_name
      
      if(grepl("PKE",trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r])){
        cond_rg_sp <- cond_rg_sp & CUs_gdb$SP_QUAL == "PKE"
      }else if(grepl("PKO",trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r])){
        cond_rg_sp <- cond_rg_sp & CUs_gdb$SP_QUAL == "PKO"
      }
      
      if(!any(cond_rg_sp)){
        print("Issue with region - species combo in CUs_gdb - BREAK")
        knitr::knit_exit()
        break
        
      }else{
        
        #' some specific case where the CU belong to two regions but is associated to 
        #' only one in CUs_gdb
        cond_exception_PKE_2236 <- grepl("PKE",trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r]) & # any(species == "Pink even") & 
          GFE_ID == 2236 # WATERBODY = DAK RIVER --> point is in Nass but CU "Nass-Skeena Estuary" only appears in Skeena
        
        if(cond_exception_PKE_2236){
          cond_rg_sp <- CUs_gdb$region_name == "Skeena" & CUs_gdb$species_name %in% species_name
        }
        
        # INTERSECTION
        layer_CU <- st_intersects(point,  CUs_gdb[cond_rg_sp,])
        
        # CUs_gdb[cond_rg_sp,][layer_CU[[1]],]
        # plot(st_geometry(regions_shp[layer_rg[[1]],]))
        # plot(st_geometry(CUs_gdb[cond_rg_sp,][layer_CU[[1]],]),add=T, col = colour_transparency_fun(c("green","blue")))
        # plot(st_geometry(point), add = T, col = "red", pch = 16, cex = 2)
        
        # plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = IndexId, GFE_IDs = GFE_ID, all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
        
        # cond_CN_3334 <- trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss$IndexId[r] == "CN_3334" # there is only one instance / GFE_IDs associated to it
        #' POPULATION = Nanaimo River Chinook (unknown timing) --> unkown timing
        #' Suggested CU: East Vancouver Island-Nanaimo & Chemainus_FA_0.x --> FALL
        #' BUT there is also potentially CU East Vancouver Island-Georgia Strait_SU_0.3 --> Summer
        #' However looking at field END_SPAWN_DT_FROM:
```
