```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=15, fig.height=15}
      if(correct_STAMP_RIVER_BELOW_FALLS){
        layout(matrix(1:2,nrow = 2))
      }
      
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = IndexId_focal,
                            GFE_ID = GFE_ID)
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- paste0("Series merged by summing to series ",IndexId_compare," & GFE_ID = ",GFE_ID," of the same CU: ",cu_name_text)
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      if(cond_CN_3334){
        print("note that the POPULATION of CN_3334 (above) shows 'unkown timing', however the field END_SPAWN_DT_FROM shows that the end of the spawning season is in the FALL, which corresonds to the correct CU:")
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds_all$IndexId %in% c("CN_3334") & all_areas_nuseds_all$GFE_ID == 1194
        show <- all_areas_nuseds_all[cond,c("IndexId","FULL_CU_IN","GFE_ID","WATERBODY","END_SPAWN_DT_FROM")] |> unique()
        rownames(show) <- NULL
        print(show)
        print("***")
      }
      
    }
    count <- count + 1
    # print("***")
  }
}  
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
sum(is.na(all_areas_nuseds_copy2$cuid)) # 2098
sum(is.na(all_areas_nuseds$cuid)) # 2085
nrow(all_areas_nuseds_copy2) # 315898
nrow(all_areas_nuseds) # 315530
```

## Issue: A same population found in mutliple locations

We now look at the case where a single `IndexId/POP_ID` is associated to multiple `GFE_ID` and make decision on a case by case basis.

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=15, fig.height=7}

all_areas_nuseds_copy3 <- all_areas_nuseds
removed_all_copy <- removed_all
added_all_copy <- added_all

# all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds_copy3
# removed_all <- removed_all_copy
# added_all <- added_all_copy

# Single `IndexId/POP_ID` associated to multiple `GFE_ID`:
  
IndexIds <- unique(all_areas_nuseds$IndexId)

# IndexIds_selected <- c()

count <- 1
for(iid in IndexIds){
  # iid <- IndexIds[1]
  # iid <- "CN_46892"
  cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == iid
  data <- unique(all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","WATERBODY","POPULATION","cuid","cu_name_pse","FULL_CU_IN")])
  
  if(nrow(data) > 1){
    
    print(paste0("*** i = ",count," ***"))
    # print(data)
    
    print(data[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","FULL_CU_IN","cuid")])
    
    plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                            GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                            all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
    
    mtext("IndexId - GFE_ID", side = 3, line = 1, adj = 0)
    mtext("POPULATION", side = 3, line = 1, adj = .5)
    mtext("WATERBODY", side = 3, line = 1, adj = 1)
    
    legend("top",data$POPULATION,bty = "n")
    legend("topright",data$WATERBODY,bty = "n")
    
    legend("bottomleft",paste("i =",count),bty = "n")
    
    if(iid == "PKO_51094"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we merge the series PKO_51094 - 489440637 to series PKO_51094 - 872 because we can assume that the two locations are in fact the same according to the WATERBODY field and the fact that no coordinates were availale for 'Atlatzi+Clear' Rivers (we defined the them manually higher in the script)."))
      
      # merge the data point
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = "PKO_51094",
                            GFE_ID = 489440637)
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- c("Series merged to alternative series IndexId = PKO_51094 & GFE_ID = 872")
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      all_areas_nuseds <- fields_edit_NUSEDS_CUSS_fun(edit_NUSEDS = T, 
                                                      IndexId_focal = "PKO_51094",
                                                      IndexId_alter = "PKO_51094",
                                                      GFE_ID_focal = 489440637,
                                                      GFE_ID_alter = 872,
                                                      all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                      conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites)
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    # if(iid == "CN_48448"){
    #   # Decision: do nothing: do not merge series CN_48448 - 443 to CN_48448 - 19723.
    # }
    
    # if(iid == "SX_47954"){
    #   # Decision: do nothing: do not merge series SX_47954 - 21 to SX_47954 - 54281.
    # }
    
    else if(iid == "CN_46892"){
      
      # cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_46892" & all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2466 & all_areas_nuseds$Year %in% c(2004,2005)
      # all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE")]
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," series CN_46892 - 290 has three duplucated data points with series CN_46892 - 2466."))
      
      print("In addition, one data point of the series seem to be a duplicate of the series CN_46891 - 290:")
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = c("CN_46892","CN_46891"), 
                              GFE_IDs = c(290,290), 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      POPULATION_here <- sapply(c("CN_46892","CN_46891"),function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond]))
      })
      
      CU_NAME_here <- sapply(c("CN_46892","CN_46891"),function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$CU_NAME[cond]))
      })
      
      mtext("POPULATION", side = 3, line = 1, adj = .5)
      legend("top",POPULATION_here,bty = "n")
      
      mtext("CU_NAME", side = 3, line = 1, adj = 1)
      legend("topright",CU_NAME_here,bty = "n")

      print("DECISION: we merge CN_46892 - 290 to series CN_46892 - 2466 after deleting the data points that are duplicated in both series CN_46892 - 2466 and CN_46891 - 290.")
      
      # series to merge: 
      cond_CN_46892_290 <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_46892" & 
        all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 290
      nuseds_CN_46892_290 <- all_areas_nuseds[cond_CN_46892_290,]
      
      # series to merge to
      cond_CN_46892_2466 <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_46892" & 
        all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2466
      nuseds_CN_46892_2466 <- all_areas_nuseds[cond_CN_46892_2466,]
      
      # remove highest data point as it is duplicated with series CN_46891 - 290
      r_toremove <- which(nuseds_CN_46892_290$MAX_ESTIMATE == max(nuseds_CN_46892_290$MAX_ESTIMATE, na.rm = T))
      nuseds_CN_46892_290 <- nuseds_CN_46892_290[-r_toremove,]
      
      # remove rows in nuseds_CN_46892_290 with duplicated values in nuseds_CN_46892_2466
      colSelect <- c("Year","MAX_ESTIMATE")
      data_here <- merge(nuseds_CN_46892_2466[,colSelect],
                         nuseds_CN_46892_290[,colSelect],
                         by = "Year", all.y = T)
      data_here <- data_here[!is.na(data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.x) &
                               !is.na(data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.y) &
                               data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.x == data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.y,]
      
      cond_keep <- ! nuseds_CN_46892_290$Year %in% data_here$Year
      nuseds_CN_46892_290 <- nuseds_CN_46892_290[cond_keep,]
      
      # remove rows in nuseds_CN_46892_2466 that NAs where nuseds_CN_46892_290 does not
      data_here <- merge(nuseds_CN_46892_2466[,colSelect],
                         nuseds_CN_46892_290[,colSelect],
                         by = "Year", all.y = T)
      data_here <- data_here[is.na(data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.x) &
                               !is.na(data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.y),]
      
      cond_keep <- ! nuseds_CN_46892_2466$Year %in% data_here$Year
      nuseds_CN_46892_2466 <- nuseds_CN_46892_2466[cond_keep,]
      
      # remove the rows in nuseds_CN_46892_290 with duplicated NAs values in nuseds_CN_46892_2466
      data_here <- merge(nuseds_CN_46892_2466[,colSelect],
                         nuseds_CN_46892_290[,colSelect],
                         by = "Year", all.y = T)
      data_here <- data_here[is.na(data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.x) &
                               is.na(data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE.y),]
      
      cond_keep <- ! nuseds_CN_46892_290$Year %in% data_here$Year
      nuseds_CN_46892_290 <- nuseds_CN_46892_290[cond_keep,]  
      
      # edit nuseds_CN_46892_290 with new GFE_ID-related fields
      for(f in fields_l$NUSEDS$GFE_ID){
        nuseds_CN_46892_290[,f] <- unique(nuseds_CN_46892_2466[,f])
      }
      
      #' edit data in all_areas_nuseds
      all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!(cond_CN_46892_290 | cond_CN_46892_2466),]
      all_areas_nuseds <- rbind(all_areas_nuseds,
                                nuseds_CN_46892_2466,
                                nuseds_CN_46892_290)
      
      # edit removed_all
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = c("CN_46892"),
                            GFE_ID = c(290))
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- "Merged to series CN_46892 & GFE_ID = 2466 with which 3 data points were duplicated; one was duplicated with CN_46891 & GFE_ID = 290"
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else if(iid == "CN_46891"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we mcan see that series CN_46891 - 2467 is a duplicate of series CN_46891 - 290, it is consequently deleted."))
      
      print("We leave series CN_46891 - 2466.")
      
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = c("CN_46891"),
                            GFE_ID = 2467)
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- "Duplicates of series IndexId = CN_46891 & GFE_ID = 290"
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      all_areas_nuseds <- remove_rows_fields_fun(dataframe = all_areas_nuseds, 
                                                 toRemove = removed, 
                                                 fields = c("IndexId","GFE_ID"))
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else if(iid == "CO_46835"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we merge the data point of series CO_46835 - 285 to series CO_46835 - 2463. Note that the two locations have the same coordinates:"))
      
      cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID %in% data$GFE_ID
      show <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[cond,c("CENSUS_SITE","GFE_ID","Y_LAT","X_LONGT")])
      rownames(show) <- NULL
      print(show)
      
      # modify NUSEDS
      all_areas_nuseds <- fields_edit_NUSEDS_CUSS_fun(edit_NUSEDS = T, 
                                                      IndexId_focal = "CO_46835",
                                                      IndexId_alter = "CO_46835",
                                                      GFE_ID_focal = 285,  # this is correct as we decided to update the alternative one
                                                      GFE_ID_alter = 2463,
                                                      all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                      conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites)
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else if(iid == "CN_46801"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we delete the data point of series CN_46801 - 33103 because it is almost a duplicate:"))
      
      cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == data$IndexId[1] & all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID %in% data$GFE_ID
      data_here <- all_areas_nuseds[cond,]
      data_here <- data_here[!is.na(data_here$MAX_ESTIMATE),]
      yr_dupli <- data_here$Year[duplicated(data_here$Year)]
      cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == data$IndexId[1] & 
        all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID %in% data$GFE_ID & 
        all_areas_nuseds$Year == yr_dupli
      show <- all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","WATERBODY","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE")]
      rownames(show) <- NULL
      print(show)
      
      # remove the point
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = c("CN_46801"),
                            GFE_ID = c(33103))
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- c("Series not merged to alternative series IndexId = CN_46801 & GFE_ID = 281 because its one data point is conflictual an almost a duplicate")
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      cond <- cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CN_46801" & all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 33103
      all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!cond,]
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else if(iid == "CO_46632"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we merge series CO_46632 - 2746 to series CO_46632 - 261 because the locations have the same coordinates:"))
      
      cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID %in% data$GFE_ID
      show <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[cond,c("CENSUS_SITE","GFE_ID","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")])
      rownames(show) <- NULL
      print(show)
      
      print("Note that no data point are in conflict.")
      
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = c("CO_46632"),
                            GFE_ID = c(2746))
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- "Series merged to series CO_46632 & GFE_ID = 2746 because same POP_ID and locations have the same coordinates."
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      all_areas_nuseds <- fields_edit_NUSEDS_CUSS_fun(edit_NUSEDS = T, 
                                                      IndexId_focal = "CO_46632",
                                                      IndexId_alter = "CO_46632",
                                                      GFE_ID_focal = 2746,
                                                      GFE_ID_alter = 261,
                                                      all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                      conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites)
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else if(iid == "CN_44577"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," there is a clear discrepancy between the series CN_44577 - 2518's POPULATION (Chandindu River) and it's WATERBODY (Wolf River) as the two rivers are very far apart."))
      
      print("But it seems that the data point in fact belongs to the series CN_44599 - 2518:")
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = c("CN_44577","CN_44599"),
                              GFE_IDs = c(2518,2518),
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      POPULATION_here <- sapply(c("CN_44577","CN_44599"),function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond]))
      })
      
      CU_NAME_here <- sapply(c("CN_44577","CN_44599"),function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$CU_NAME[cond]))
      })
      
      mtext("POPULATION", side = 3, line = 1, adj = .5)
      legend("top",POPULATION_here,bty = "n")
      
      mtext("CU_NAME", side = 3, line = 1, adj = 1)
      legend("topright",CU_NAME_here,bty = "n")
      
      print("We decide to merge the data point to CN_44599 - 2518:")
      
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = "CN_44577",
                            GFE_ID = 2518)
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- c("Data point merge series IndexId = CN_44599 & GFE_ID = 2518 because of mismatch in POPULATION vs. WATERBODY")
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      all_areas_nuseds <- fields_edit_NUSEDS_CUSS_fun(edit_NUSEDS = T, 
                                                      IndexId_focal = "CN_44577",
                                                      IndexId_alter = "CN_44599",
                                                      GFE_ID_focal = 2518,
                                                      GFE_ID_alter = 2518,
                                                      all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                      conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites)
      
      # need to update CU_NAME because it was 
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = c("CN_44577","CN_44599"),
                              GFE_IDs = c(2518,2518),
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else if(iid == "SX_3416"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we merge series SX_3416 - 2746 to series SX_3416 - 261 because the locations have the same coordinates:"))
      
      cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID %in% data$GFE_ID
      show <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[cond,c("CENSUS_SITE","GFE_ID","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")])
      rownames(show) <- NULL
      print(show)

      print("Note that no data point are in conflict.")
      
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = c("SX_3416"),
                            GFE_ID = c(2746))
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- "Series not in CUSS, merged to series SX_3416 & GFE_ID = 261  because same POP_ID and locations have the same coordinates."
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      all_areas_nuseds <- fields_edit_NUSEDS_CUSS_fun(edit_NUSEDS = T, 
                                                      IndexId_focal = "SX_3416",
                                                      IndexId_alter = "SX_3416",
                                                      GFE_ID_focal = 2746,
                                                      GFE_ID_alter = 261,
                                                      all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds,
                                                      conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites)
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else if(iid == "SX_3302"){
      #' Cases with SX_3302 --> do nothing but show that there is probably some mistakes here
      
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we see that there are multiple time series that could potentially be merged (see both above and below); we however do not make any change."))
      
      IndexIds <- c("SX_3302","SX_3310","SX_3325","SX_3302")
      GFE_IDs <- c(3444,3416,3444,3416)
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = IndexIds,
                              GFE_IDs = GFE_IDs,
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      # WATERBODY_here <- sapply(GFE_IDs,function(x){
      #   cond <- all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == x
      #   return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$WATERBODY[cond]))
      # })
      
      POPULATION_here <- sapply(IndexIds,function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond]))
      })
      
      CU_NAME_here <- sapply(IndexIds,function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$CU_NAME[cond]))
      })
      
      mtext("POPULATION", side = 3, line = 1, adj = .5)
      legend("top",POPULATION_here,bty = "n")
      
      mtext("CU_NAME", side = 3, line = 1, adj = 1)
      legend("topright",CU_NAME_here,bty = "n")
    }
    
    else if(iid == "CN_2178"){
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","BEFORE"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
      
      m <- paste0("In case i = ",count," above, the series CN_2178 - 129 is a duplicate of the series CN_47189 - 129 as we can see below:")
      print(m)     
      
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = c(data$IndexId[1],"CN_47189"), 
                              GFE_IDs = c(129,129), 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      POPULATION_here <- sapply(c(data$IndexId[1],"CN_47189"), function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$POPULATION[cond]))
      })
      
      CU_NAME_here <- sapply(c(data$IndexId[1],"CN_47189"), function(x){
        cond <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == x
        return(unique(all_areas_nuseds$CU_NAME[cond]))
      })
      
      mtext("POPULATION", side = 3, line = 1, adj = .5)
      legend("top",POPULATION_here,bty = "n")
      
      mtext("CU_NAME", side = 3, line = 1, adj = 1)
      legend("topright",CU_NAME_here,bty = "n")
      
      print("The series is consequently removed from NUSEDS.")
    
      removed <- data.frame(IndexId = c("CN_2178"),
                            GFE_ID = 129)
      removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
      removed$comment <- "Duplicates of series IndexId = CN_47189 & GFE_ID = 129"
      removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)
      
      all_areas_nuseds <- remove_rows_fields_fun(dataframe = all_areas_nuseds, 
                                                 toRemove = removed, 
                                                 fields = c("IndexId","GFE_ID"))
      
      plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                              GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                              all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
      
      legend("topleft",c(rep("",length(data$POPULATION)),"","AFTER"),bty = "n",text.font = 2)
    }
    
    else{
      print(paste0("In case i = ",count," we leave the data as is."))
    }
    
    count <- count + 1
    
    # IndexIds_selected <- c(IndexIds_selected,iid)
  }
}  

# length(IndexIds_selected)
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
sum(is.na(all_areas_nuseds_copy3$cuid)) # 2085
sum(is.na(all_areas_nuseds$cuid)) # 2085
nrow(all_areas_nuseds_copy3) # 315530
nrow(all_areas_nuseds) # 315510
```

## Extra corrections for TBR

PSF formed a technical working group (TWG) specifically to compile data for the Transboundary region (cf. [transboundary-data](https://github.com/salmonwatersheds/transboundary-data/tree/main)). As part of the work the following modifications were requested:

- `POP_ID = 45144`: Revise name of sockeye survey from Tahltan River to Tahltan Lake (Jason Calvert, pers. comm. January 9, 2025)

- TATSAMENIE RIVER coho (`POP_ID = 45152`) for 1994 and earlier changed to TATSATUA RIVER (`POP_ID = 45154`) and remaining records 1995+ are removed

- any records of `POP_ID = 45151` (Tatsamenie River lake-type sockeye) for 1994 or earlier get changed to `POP_ID = 45153` (TATSATUA RIVER river-type sockeye)

- one record with `POP_ID = 45165` (Chinook Run 2) change to `45164` (Chinook Run 1) in Nahlin river

```{r, include=FALSE}
# Sources:

# file:///C:/Users/bcarturan/Salmon%20Watersheds%20Dropbox/Bruno%20Carturan/X%20Drive/1_PROJECTS/1_Active/Population%20Methods%20and%20Analysis/population-data/transboundary-data/README.html

# https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/5m93l1whnuqtcvi3h9rh0/NuSEDS_modifications_hardcode.docx?rlkey=c1fykonhk1cdvdg0i8aizsgkc&e=1&dl=0


all_areas_nuseds_copy4 <- all_areas_nuseds
# all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds_copy4
```

We execute and document these changes below.

- `POP_ID = 45144`: Revise name of sockeye survey from Tahltan River to Tahltan Lake (Jason Calvert, pers. comm. January 9, 2025)

```{r, echo=FALSE}
#' POP_ID = 45144: Revise name of sockeye survey from Tahltan River to Tahltan Lake (Jason Calvert, pers. comm. January 9, 2025)
cond <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID) & all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID == 45144
show <- all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("IndexId","cuid",'POPULATION',"WATERBODY")] |> unique()
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
```

The name was already changed in NuSEDS so nothing to do.

- TATSAMENIE RIVER coho (`POP_ID = 45152`) for 1994 and earlier changed to TATSATUA RIVER (`POP_ID = 45154`) and remaining records 1995+ are removed

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12}
#' TODO: TATSAMENIE RIVER coho (POP_ID = 45152) for 1994 and earlier changed to 
#' TATSATUA RIVER (45154) and remaining records 1995+ are removed.
cond <- !is.na(all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID) & all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID %in% c(45152,45154)
data <- all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("cu_name_pse","cuid","IndexId",'POPULATION',"WATERBODY",
                                "GFE_ID")] |> unique()
rownames(data) <- NULL
data

plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                        GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                        all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
legend("top",data$POPULATION,bty = "n")
legend("topright",data$WATERBODY,bty = "n")
mtext("POPULATION",side = 3,line = 1, adj = .5)
mtext("WATERBODY",side = 3,line = 1, adj = 1)
mtext(data$cu_name_pse[2],side = 3,line = 1, adj = 0)
# mtext(data$cu_name_pse[1],side = 3,line = 2, adj = 0)
mtext("cu_name_pse:",side = 3,line = 2, adj = 0)

#' !!! PROBLEM !!! --> POP_ID 45154 is already associated to another GFE_ID
```

**ISSUE**: as we can see above, `POP_ID 45154` is already associated to another `GFE_ID = 2212`, which corresponds to a location with the same `WATERBODY` but different coordinates:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID %in% data$GFE_ID
show <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[cond,c("GFE_ID","CENSUS_SITE","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")])
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
# GFE_ID    CENSUS_SITE     X_LONGT      Y_LAT
#   2212 TATSATUA CREEK -132.142956 58.5347378 --> in the Tatsatua Lake
#   2211 TATSATUA CREEK -132.394879 58.4400493 --> at the mouth of the river
```

`GFE_ID = 2212` is in the Tatsatua Lake whereas `GFE_ID = 2211` is at the mouth of the river.

**DECISION**

- For year <= 1994: replace `POP_ID = 45152` by `45154` BUT keep `GFE_ID = 2211` (note that `POP_ID = 45151` is then associated to two `GFE_ID`, which is not ideal, but at least we preserve the time series in blue above)

- For year > 1994: delete

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.width=12}
#' DECISION:
#' - for year <= 1994: replace POP_ID 45152 --> 45154 BUT keep GFE_ID = 2211
#' - for year > 1994: delete 

removed <- data.frame(IndexId = "CO_45152",GFE_ID = 2211)
removed$dataset <- "NUSEDS"
removed$comment <- c("Manual correction: CO_45152 & 2211 --> CO_45154 & 2211 year <= 1994, rest of points deleted")
removed_all <- rbind(removed_all,removed)

var_IndexId <- c("IndexId","POP_ID","POPULATION")

cond_focal <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CO_45152" &
  all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2211

cond_alter <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "CO_45154" &
  all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2212
# 
# yr_min <- min(c(min(all_areas_nuseds$Year[cond_focal]),
#                 min(all_areas_nuseds$Year[cond_alter])))
# 
# yr_max <- max(c(max(all_areas_nuseds$Year[cond_focal]),
#                 max(all_areas_nuseds$Year[cond_alter])))
# 
# count_min <- min(c(min(all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE[cond_focal],na.rm = T),
#                 min(all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE[cond_alter],na.rm = T)))
# 
# count_max <- max(c(max(all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE[cond_focal],na.rm = T),
#                    max(all_areas_nuseds$MAX_ESTIMATE[cond_alter],na.rm = T)))

cond_yr <- all_areas_nuseds$Year <= 1994

for(c in c("IndexId","POP_ID","POPULATION","RUN_TYPE")){ # RUN_TYPE = "" for both
  all_areas_nuseds[cond_focal & cond_yr,c] <- all_areas_nuseds[cond_alter,c] |> unique()
}

# remove the data points > 1994
all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!(cond_focal & !cond_yr),]


# check
plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = c(data$IndexId,"CO_45154"), 
                        GFE_IDs = c(data$GFE_ID,2211), 
                        all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
legend("top",data$POPULATION,bty = "n")
legend("topright",data$WATERBODY,bty = "n")
mtext("POPULATION",side = 3,line = 1, adj = .5)
mtext("WATERBODY",side = 3,line = 1, adj = 1)
mtext(data$cu_name_pse[2],side = 3,line = 1, adj = 0)
# mtext(data$cu_name_pse[1],side = 3,line = 2, adj = 0)
mtext("cu_name_pse:",side = 3,line = 2, adj = 0)
```

Any we have to add the reference combing `POP_ID = 45154` and `GFE_ID = 2211` to **CUSS**:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cuss_new <- CUSS_newRow_fun(IndexId = "CO_45154", 
                            GFE_ID = 2211, 
                            conservation_unit_system_sites = conservation_unit_census_sites,
                            all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)

cuss_new$cuid <- unique(data$cuid)
cuss_new$cu_name_pse <- unique(data$cu_name_pse)

conservation_unit_census_sites <- rbind(conservation_unit_census_sites,cuss_new)

cuss_new
```

- Any records of `POP_ID = 45151` (Tatsamenie River lake-type sockeye) for 1994 or earlier get changed to `POP_ID = 45153` (TATSATUA RIVER river-type sockeye)

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12}
#' TODO: any records of 45151 (SEL) for 1994 or earlier get changed to 45153 (TATSATUA RIVER river-type sockeye)
cond <- all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID %in% c(45151,45153)
data <- all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("cu_name_pse","cuid","IndexId",'POPULATION',"WATERBODY","GFE_ID")] |> unique()
row.names(data) <- NULL

data

plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                        GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                        all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
# legend("top",data$POPULATION,bty = "n")
legend("topright",data$WATERBODY,bty = "n")
mtext("POPULATION",side = 3,line = 1, adj = .5)
mtext("WATERBODY",side = 3,line = 1, adj = 1)

SPECIES_QUALIFIED <- sapply(data$IndexId,function(x){
  cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$IndexId == x
  return(unique(conservation_unit_census_sites$SPECIES_QUALIFIED[cond]))
})
legend("top",paste0(data$POPULATION," (",SPECIES_QUALIFIED,")"),bty = "n")
# mtext(data$SPECIES_QUALIFIED[2],side = 3,line = 1, adj = 0)
# mtext(data$SPECIES_QUALIFIED[1],side = 3,line = 2, adj = 0)

# cond <- conservation_unit_census_sites$GFE_ID %in% data$GFE_ID
# unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[cond,c("GFE_ID","CENSUS_SITE","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")])
# GFE_ID    CENSUS_SITE     X_LONGT      Y_LAT
#  2212 TATSATUA CREEK -132.142956 58.5347378 --> at the mouth of the river
#  2211 TATSATUA CREEK -132.394879 58.4400493 --> in the Tatsatua Lake
```

**ISSUE**: `POP_ID = 45153` is already associated to another `GFE_ID = 2212`. However, the data points are almost duplicated. Noe that the `ESTIMATE_METHOD` for the alternative series in blue is "Unknown Estimate Method":

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# !!! PROBLEM !!! --> POP_ID 45153 is already associated to another GFE_ID

# Check the counts for the overlapping years
cond_focal <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "SX_45151" &
  all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2211

cond_alter <- all_areas_nuseds$IndexId == "SX_45153" &
   all_areas_nuseds$GFE_ID == 2212

cond_yr <- all_areas_nuseds$Year <= 1994
check <- merge(x = all_areas_nuseds[cond_focal & cond_yr,c("IndexId","WATERBODY","MAX_ESTIMATE","Year","ESTIMATE_METHOD")],
              y = all_areas_nuseds[cond_alter & cond_yr,c("IndexId","WATERBODY","MAX_ESTIMATE","Year","ESTIMATE_METHOD")],
              by = c("WATERBODY","Year"),all = T)

check <- check[order(check$Year),]
row.names(check) <- NULL
check
#' the counts for SX_45151 are "Fence Count" vs. "Unknown" for SX_45151 --> so better
#' keep this data considering they are almost the same.
```

**DECISION**

- For years <= 1994: change `POP_ID = 45151` to `POP_ID = 45153` and `GFE_ID = 2211` to `GFE_ID = 2212`

- Delete the existing series `POP_ID = 45153` and `GFE_ID = 2212`

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12}

# all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds_copy4

#' DECISION:
#' - for years <= 1994: change IndexId from 45151 to 45153 and GFE_ID from 2211 to 2212
#' - delete series POP_ID = SX_45153 and GFE_ID = 2212

for(c in c("IndexId","POP_ID","POPULATION","RUN_TYPE","SPECIES","species_acronym_ncc","cuid","cu_name_pse","FULL_CU_IN","CU_NAME")){
  # c <- "FULL_CU_IN"
  all_areas_nuseds[cond_focal & cond_yr,c] <- all_areas_nuseds[cond_alter,c] |> unique()
}

col_GFE_ID <-  c("GFE_ID","WATERSHED_CDE","FWA_WATERSHED_CDE","WATERBODY",               
                 "GAZETTED_NAME","LOCAL_NAME_1","LOCAL_NAME_2","AREA")

for(c in col_GFE_ID){
  # c <- col_GFE_ID[1]
  all_areas_nuseds[cond_focal & cond_yr,c] <- all_areas_nuseds[cond_alter,c] |> unique()
}

# delete the rest of the alternative time series
all_areas_nuseds <- all_areas_nuseds[!(cond_alter & cond_yr),]

# check
plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = data$IndexId, 
                        GFE_IDs = data$GFE_ID, 
                        all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
legend("topright",data$WATERBODY,bty = "n")
mtext("POPULATION",side = 3,line = 1, adj = .5)
mtext("WATERBODY",side = 3,line = 1, adj = 1)
legend("top",paste0(data$POPULATION," (",SPECIES_QUALIFIED,")"),bty = "n")
```

- One record with `POP_ID = 45165` (Chinook Run 2) change to `45164` (Chinook Run 1) in Nahlin river

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12}
#' one record with 45165 change to 45164 (NAHLIN Chinook)
#' 
cond <- all_areas_nuseds$POP_ID %in% c(45165,45164)
show <- all_areas_nuseds[cond,c("IndexId","cuid",'POPULATION',"WATERBODY","GFE_ID")] |> unique()
rownames(show) <- NULL
show

plot_IndexId_GFE_ID_fun(IndexIds = c("CN_45164","CN_45165"), 
                        GFE_IDs = c(2224,2224), 
                        all_areas_nuseds = all_areas_nuseds)
```

Looks like the change was done in the process above, as shown in our records:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cond <- removed_all$IndexId == "CN_45165"
show <- removed_all[cond,]
rownames(show) <- NULL
show
# --> ALL GOOD, already done in 1_nuseds_collation.rmd
```

# Merge NUSEDS and CUSS & checks

Merge:

```{r}
col_common <- colnames(all_areas_nuseds)[colnames(all_areas_nuseds) %in% colnames(conservation_unit_census_sites)]
col_common <- col_common[col_common != "species_acronym_ncc"]

col_nuseds <- colnames(all_areas_nuseds)[!colnames(all_areas_nuseds) %in% colnames(conservation_unit_census_sites)]

col_cuss <- colnames(conservation_unit_census_sites)[!colnames(conservation_unit_census_sites) %in% colnames(all_areas_nuseds)]

nuseds_final <- base::merge(x = all_areas_nuseds[,c(col_common,col_nuseds)], 
                            y = conservation_unit_census_sites[,c(col_common,col_cuss)], 
                            by = col_common, 
                            all.x = T)

```

```{r, include=FALSE}
nrow(nuseds_final)     # 315501 315510 312930 311957 310644 310644 310538 309727 307217
nrow(all_areas_nuseds) # 315501 315510 312930 311957 310644 310644 310538 309727 307217
nrow(conservation_unit_census_sites) # 7409 7408 7036 6955
```

There should not be any duplicate years, is that true?

```{r, echo = FALSE}
cols_NuSEDS <- c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year")
data <- nuseds_final
dupli <- duplicated(data[,cols_NuSEDS])

if(any(dupli)){
  print("NO :-(")
  show <- data[dupli,cols_NuSEDS] |>
  arrange(POP_ID,Year)
  print(show)
  
  for(r in 1:nrow(show)){
    cond <- data$IndexId == show$IndexId[r] &
      data$GFE_ID == show$GFE_ID[r] &
      data$Year == show$Year[r]
    show_here <- data[cond,c("IndexId","GFE_ID","Year","MAX_ESTIMATE","ESTIMATE_METHOD")]
    print(show_here)
  }
}else{
  print("YES :-)")
}
```

All series in **NUSEDS** should be in **CUSS**, is that true?

```{r,echo = F}
series_nuseds <- unique(all_areas_nuseds[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])
series_nuseds <- paste(series_nuseds$IndexId,series_nuseds$GFE_ID,sep="&")

series_cuss <- unique(conservation_unit_census_sites[,c("IndexId","GFE_ID")])
series_cuss <- paste(series_cuss$IndexId,series_cuss$GFE_ID,sep="&")

if(all(series_nuseds %in% series_cuss)){
  print("YES :-)")
}else{
  print("NO :-(")
}
```

All locations have coordinates, is that true?

```{r, echo=FALSE}
locations <- unique(nuseds_final[,c("GFE_ID","X_LONGT","Y_LAT")])

cond_NA <- is.na(locations$X_LONGT) | is.na(locations$Y_LAT)

if(any(cond_NA)){
  print("NO :-(")
}else{
  print("YES :-)")
}
```

And a same location does not has different coordinates. Is that true?

```{r, echo=FALSE}
GFE_IDs <- unique(nuseds_final[,c("GFE_ID")])

if(nrow(locations) > length(GFE_IDs)){
  print("NO :-(")
}else{
  print("YES :-)")
}
```

No series are missing a CU-association (i.e. missing both a `FULL_CU_IN` and a `cuid`), correct?

```{r, echo=FALSE}
cond <- nuseds_final$FULL_CU_IN == "" & is.na(nuseds_final$cuid)

if(any(cond)){
  print("NO :-(")
}else{
  print("YES :-)")
}
```

No `IndeId/POP_ID` is associated to multiple CUs, correct?

```{r, echo=FALSE}
IndexId <- unique(nuseds_final$IndexId)
IndexId_cu <- unique(nuseds_final[,c("IndexId","CU_NAME","FULL_CU_IN","cuid","cu_name_pse")])

if(nrow(IndexId_cu) != length(IndexId)){
  print("NO :-(")
}else{
  print("YES :-)")
}
```

# Export datasets

The following files are exported:

-   *1_NuSEDS_escapement_data_collated_DATE.csv*: the cleaned combined **NUSEDS** and **CUSS** datasets

-   *1_series_inNUSEDS_noInCUSS_DATE.csv*: information about the series in **NUSEDS** and not in **CUSS**

-   *1_series_removed_DATE.csv*: the series removed from either **NUSEDS** or **CUSS** and why

-   *1_series_added_DATE.csv*: the series added to either **NUSEDS** or **CUSS** and why

-   *log_file.csv*: the log file reporting the name of the main file exported, the date of the export, the name of the present script, the name of the original **NUSEDS** or **CUSS** files and the choices related to removing zeros or not.

```{r, include=FALSE}
if(export_datasets){
  
  date <- as.character(Sys.Date())
  
  # Export the merged and cleaned NuSEDS data
  name_file <- paste0("1_NuSEDS_escapement_data_collated_",date)
  write.csv(nuseds_final,paste0(wd_output,"/archive/",name_file,".csv"),
            row.names = F)
  
  #' Export the series in NUSEDS with IndexIds and GFE_IDs not in CUSS with 
  #' info on the number of data points and POPULATION
  #' slack: https://salmonwatersheds.slack.com/archives/CJ5RVHVCG/p1709250704372429
  write.csv(trackRecord_nuseds_nocuss,paste0(wd_output,"/archive/1_series_inNUSEDS_noInCUSS_",date,".csv"),
            row.names = F)
  
  #' Export the CUs removed and those added:
  removed_all$POP_ID <- sapply(X = removed_all$IndexId, 
                               FUN = function(iid){strsplit(x = iid,split = "_")[[1]][2]})
  
  removed_all$species_acro <- sapply(X = removed_all$IndexId, 
                                     FUN = function(iid){strsplit(x = iid,split = "_")[[1]][1]}) 
  
  removed_all <- removed_all[,c("IndexId","POP_ID","species_acro","GFE_ID","dataset","comment")]
  
  write.csv(removed_all,paste0(wd_output,"/archive/1_series_removed_",date,".csv"),row.names = F)
  
  #
  added_all$POP_ID <- sapply(X = added_all$IndexId, 
                             FUN = function(iid){strsplit(x = iid,split = "_")[[1]][2]})
  
  added_all$species_acro <- sapply(X = added_all$IndexId, 
                                   FUN = function(iid){strsplit(x = iid,split = "_")[[1]][1]}) 
  
  added_all <- added_all[,c("IndexId","POP_ID","species_acro","CU_NAME","GFE_ID","dataset","comment")]
  
  
  write.csv(added_all,paste0(wd_output,"/archive/1_series_added_",date,".csv"),row.names = F)
  
  
  # Edit the log file locally (needs to be pushed to github)
  logfile_path <- paste0(getwd(),"/spawner-surveys/output/log_file.csv")
  logfile_path <- gsub("spawner-surveys/code/","",logfile_path)
  log_file <- read.csv(logfile_path,header = T)
  
  log_file_new <- log_file[1,]
  log_file_new$main_dataset_exported <- name_file
  log_file_new$R_script <- "1_nuseds_collation.rmd"
  log_file_new$date_export <- date
  log_file_new$all_areas_nuseds_file_name <- all_areas_nuseds_file_name
  log_file_new$conservation_unit_system_sites_file_name <- conservation_unit_census_sites_file_name
  log_file_new$remove_timeSeries_zeros_too <- remove_timeSeries_zeros_too
  log_file_new$replace_zeros_byNAs <- F # replace_zeros_byNAs
  
  log_file <- rbind(log_file,log_file_new)
  
  wd_output_local <- getwd()
  wd_output_local <- gsub("code","output",wd_output_local)
  
  write.csv(log_file,paste0(wd_output_local,"/log_file.csv"),row.names = F)
  
}
```
